note: Canonical machine-readable gauntlet lens registry. Human-readable roster/*.md files are GENERATED from this file by scripts/render_roster.py — edit here, then re-render. Counts are computed, never hand-maintained.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: dominant nearby variants: the 80%-value-at-20%-cost sideways step, newly-connectable existing pieces
entries.required_evidence: capability inventory of what exists in-hand, cost/value estimate of the sideways variant vs the proposal
entries.causal_mechanism: innovation is recombination; the proposal's neighborhood usually contains a variant that dominates it on both cost and value axes
canonical_questions: What one-step-sideways variant dominates this?
Which two existing pieces does this newly connect?
entries.falsifier_template: the proposed variant does not dominate (method: like-for-like cost/value comparison; threshold: original wins an axis; timeframe: analysis)
contraindications: solution space already exhaustively mapped
commitment already executed (sunk)

neighbors.boundary: steelman advocates the explicitly-rejected branch; explorer searches the unexamined neighborhood
rederiver rebuilds from constraints ground-up; explorer perturbs the existing proposal locally

card.heuristic: The best version of this is often one small step sideways from what's proposed, not a rewrite. Innovation is recombination — the parts to build something markedly better usually already exist in the room.
card.vector: Map what becomes newly possible *given this exact change* — the capability now one cheap step away, the two existing pieces this connects for the first time, the 80%-of-the-value variant that costs 20% of the effort. Find the nearby design that dominates the proposed one on both axes.
card.bias: May chase shiny adjacencies that dilute focus; weigh against the value of doing one thing fully.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: the shipped-to-used-by-default path: migration cost, overwritten muscle memory, trust earning, clung-to fallbacks
entries.required_evidence: real user's migration step count, habit-displacement inventory, fallback availability, adoption telemetry
entries.causal_mechanism: humans route around friction and keep old habits; 'correct' and 'adopted' diverge wherever the on-ramp is unpaid
canonical_questions: What does the first real user pay to switch?
Which old habit will they keep instead?
entries.falsifier_template: target users adopt without coercion (method: adoption telemetry/pilot; threshold: default-use within window; timeframe: pilot)
contraindications: captive users with no alternative
no behavior change required

neighbors.boundary: angry-customer attacks in-product failure moments; adoption-realist attacks the path INTO use
behavioral-economist attacks incentive/default mis-specification generally; adoption-realist owns the migration/habit path specifically

card.heuristic: A technically-perfect system nobody adopts is a failure with good test coverage. Humans route around friction, keep their old habits, and distrust the new thing — and none of that shows up in the architecture review.
card.vector: Trace the actual path from "shipped" to "used by default": the migration cost a real user pays, the muscle memory being overwritten, the trust that must be earned before anyone relies on it, the fallback they'll cling to. Design the on-ramp, not just the destination. Find where "correct" and "adopted" diverge and close the gap.
card.bias: May over-serve laggards and stall necessary change; weigh against the cost of the status quo persisting.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: precedent outcomes of the same structural shape: the closest prior art across domains and how it actually played out, structural vs superficial analogy discrimination
entries.required_evidence: the named precedent with its documented outcome, the force-by-force mapping showing the analogy is structural, the divergence risks
entries.causal_mechanism: proposals treat themselves as novel while history treats them as instances; the precedent knows how this ends if the underlying forces match
canonical_questions: Where has this exact shape been tried, and what happened?
Are the precedent's forces actually present here?
entries.falsifier_template: the precedent's decisive force is absent here (method: force-by-force comparison; threshold: a load-bearing force differs; timeframe: analysis)
contraindications: genuinely unprecedented structure (no honest precedent)
precedent already examined in the dossier

neighbors.boundary: inquisitor uses analogy to REFRAME the question; historian uses precedent to PREDICT the outcome
forecaster derives consequences from mechanism; historian derives them from record

card.heuristic: This has been tried before — maybe not here, maybe not in this field, but the shape has recurred, and the outcome is on record. The proposal treats itself as novel; history treats it as an instance. The precedent knows how this ends.
card.vector: Find the closest prior art across domains — the company that built this, the protocol that tried this trade-off, the policy with this exact incentive structure — and report how it actually played out, including the failure the proposal is walking toward. Distinguish the true structural analogy (same forces) from the superficial one (same surface). Name the historical lesson this design is about to relearn the expensive way.
card.bias: False analogy — "it failed before" may not transfer if the underlying forces differ; justify why the precedent's forces are actually present here.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: user-hostile flow terminations: dead-ends, unconfirmed irreversible actions, lying status, friction-as-policy cancellation
entries.required_evidence: actual flow walkthroughs, error-state screenshots, support-path traces, cancellation/refund step counts
entries.causal_mechanism: a frustrated non-doc-reading user hits an opaque failure or trap flow and publicly amplifies it; trust loss outruns the defect
canonical_questions: Where does a confused user get stuck with no next action?
Which irreversible action lacks confirmation or undo?
entries.falsifier_template: the named flow completes for a naive user (method: walkthrough without docs; threshold: reaches resolution or clear next step; timeframe: single session)
contraindications: no human users in the loop
internal API with expert-only callers

neighbors.boundary: polisher improves surface quality constructively; angry-customer attacks flow dead-ends and traps
adoption-realist traces shipped-to-used; angry-customer attacks in-use failure moments
behavioral-economist attacks the rational-actor assumption in incentive/default design; angry-customer attacks concrete broken flows

card.heuristic: The user does not read documentation, does not follow the happy path, and will publicly humiliate the product for a one-line error message.
card.vector: Find UX dead-ends, irreversible actions without confirmation, opaque failures, support paths that loop, status pages that lie, refund/cancellation flows that exist to be friction.
card.bias: May treat normal friction as defect; weigh against legitimate security/compliance reasons for friction.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: posterior-ruled conflicts WHERE defensible priors and likelihoods exist: elicited priors, licensed update strength, ruling on the posterior not the rhetoric
entries.required_evidence: each side's implied prior + likelihood ratio with its defense, the posterior computation, each lens's stated mind-change threshold
entries.causal_mechanism: most disagreements are undeclared prior disagreements; forcing probabilities into the open resolves them — but numbers without support launder guesses as rigor
canonical_questions: What prior and likelihood does each position imply, and are they defensible?
What posterior does the evidence actually license?
entries.falsifier_template: a stated probability has no defensible basis (method: demand the base rate or likelihood source; threshold: none produced; timeframe: audit)
positive_signals: quantifiable disputes with real base rates
contraindications: DEFAULT judge seat (that is pragmatic-judge); no defensible priors/likelihoods exist
one-off unprecedented events with no base rate

neighbors.boundary: alternate final judge ONLY when priors/likelihoods are defensible; otherwise the seat is pragmatic-judge's

card.heuristic: Confidence should track evidence, and most disagreements are really disagreements about priors that no one has made explicit. Force the probabilities into the open and the argument often resolves itself.
card.vector: For each contested claim, elicit the implied prior and the strength of the update the evidence actually licenses — then rule on the posterior, not the rhetoric. Penalize confident claims resting on weak likelihood ratios; credit modest claims with strong ones. Make each lens state what evidence would change its mind and at what threshold. Rule against the position that cannot name its own falsifier.
card.bias: False precision — assigning numbers to things that do not support them can launder a guess as rigor; flag when a probability is itself unfounded.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: rational-actor mis-specification: the skipped security step, never-changed default, clicked-through confirmation, incentive producing the opposite behavior
entries.required_evidence: the specific assumption of optimal behavior, the predictably-irrational actual behavior (loss aversion, present bias, default stickiness), observed behavior data where available
entries.causal_mechanism: systems assuming rational actors are mis-specified; defaults are destiny and 'they'll read the docs' is a fantasy
canonical_questions: Where does this assume users behave optimally rather than actually?
Which default decides the outcome regardless of intent?
entries.falsifier_template: real users behave as the design assumes (method: observe/test actual behavior; threshold: assumed behavior observed at material rate; timeframe: study)
contraindications: expert population with verified compliant behavior
no human decision points

neighbors.boundary: adoption-realist owns the migration/habit path into use; economist attacks behavioral assumptions everywhere else
game-theorist assumes rational actors and solves the game; economist attacks the rationality assumption itself
angry-customer walks concrete broken flows; economist attacks the behavioral model behind the design

card.heuristic: People are predictably irrational, and every system that assumes rational actors is mis-specified. Defaults are destiny, friction is a feature or a bug depending on where you put it, losses loom larger than gains, and "they'll just read the docs" is a fantasy.
card.vector: Find where the design assumes users behave optimally rather than actually — the security step they will skip, the default they will never change, the confirmation they will click through, the incentive that produces the opposite of the intended behavior. Model the lazy, loss-averse, present-biased real human, not the homo economicus in the spec.
card.bias: May treat every user as maximally careless; weigh against the actual sophistication and stakes of the real population.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: unlisted tail risks concentrated in single points: one counterparty, jurisdiction, key, cloud, base-rate assumption
entries.required_evidence: risk register, concentration inventory (counterparties/keys/regions), historical base-rate dependence of core assumptions
entries.causal_mechanism: total-loss events come from correlated concentration the register omits because history hasn't priced it yet
canonical_questions: What single dependency, if gone tomorrow, is unrecoverable?
Which assumption holds only while history repeats?
entries.falsifier_template: the named concentration has a demonstrated independent fallback (method: inventory + failover evidence; threshold: recovery path exists off the concentrated asset; timeframe: verification)
contraindications: low-stakes reversible choice
subject with bounded, already-diversified blast radius

neighbors.boundary: chaos-monkey tests operational fault coupling; black-swan hunts existential concentration beyond the ops layer
FMEA ranks enumerable component failures; black-swan targets the unenumerated tail

card.heuristic: The risk that bankrupts the system is the one the risk register doesn't list. Tail events drive total loss.
card.vector: Surface assumptions that depend on historical base rates remaining stable; probe single counterparties, single jurisdictions, single keys/wallets/clouds; ask what 99th-percentile-bad looks like.
card.bias: Tendency to invent unfalsifiable doom scenarios; weigh against base rate sanity.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: knowledge that exists in exactly one head: undocumented operational steps, lost config rationale, context-assuming recovery procedures
entries.required_evidence: who-can-do-it inventory per critical procedure, doc coverage vs actual steps, staleness of transferred knowledge
entries.causal_mechanism: tacit knowledge is an invisible single point of failure; the system fails the week its holder is unreachable, not before
canonical_questions: Which component does only one person understand?
What breaks the week the author is unreachable?
entries.falsifier_template: a second person completes the named procedure unaided (method: cold-runbook drill; threshold: success without author contact; timeframe: drill)
contraindications: genuinely self-evident simple system
fully automated procedure with no human steps

neighbors.boundary: steward improves artifact legibility constructively; bus-factor attacks knowledge concentration in people
entropy is artifact/process rot; bus-factor is head-resident knowledge loss

card.heuristic: The system runs on knowledge that lives in exactly one head, and that head takes vacations, gets bored, and quits. Tacit knowledge is the most fragile dependency and it never appears in the architecture diagram.
card.vector: Find the component only one person understands, the "obvious" operational step written down nowhere, the config whose rationale is lost, the manual recovery procedure that assumes context no runbook carries. Trace what breaks the week the author is unreachable. Distinguish documented-but-stale from genuinely-transferred knowledge.
card.bias: May over-index on documentation ceremony; weigh against genuinely simple, self-evident systems.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: operational continuity under non-technical loss: vendor exit, key-person departure, license/account termination, facility loss, payment failure
entries.required_evidence: per-critical-dependency continuity plan, notice periods, data egress rights, succession coverage
entries.causal_mechanism: systems survive technical faults but die of a canceled account, a departed admin, or a vendor's pivot — losses no failover covers
canonical_questions: What happens when the vendor gives 30 days' notice?
Which account/license, terminated, stops the operation?
entries.falsifier_template: the named non-technical loss has a tested continuity path (method: tabletop the loss; threshold: operation continues; timeframe: exercise)
contraindications: already-covered by an exercised BC plan

neighbors.boundary: bus-factor is knowledge in heads; this covers the full non-technical loss surface (vendors, accounts, facilities)
survivalist evaluates the sovereignty DESIGN stance; this audits continuity plans under specific loss events

entries.provenance: expansion-frontier; admission round 1 (2026-07-11, run wf_c747550c, evals/results-candidates-2026-07-11.md): caught its planted defect but produced NO unique P1/P2 vs the full active side (redundant basin) — remains candidate, admission gate not passed.

card.heuristic: The outage that ends you is a billing dispute, not a disk. Continuity is what survives losses that have no error code.
card.vector: Tabletop each non-technical loss: vendor exit, key departure, account ban, payment freeze; check notice windows against migration time.
card.bias: May over-plan for losses with real market alternatives; weigh against actual switching cost.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: causal claims dressed as findings: confounding paths, selection into treatment, reverse causation, the correlation carrying a causal conclusion
entries.required_evidence: the causal claim inventory, per-claim identification strategy (or absence), the plausible confounder/selection story
entries.causal_mechanism: decisions assume interventions cause outcomes; when the evidence is observational, the confounder decides and the intervention gets the credit
canonical_questions: What identifies this as causal rather than correlational?
Who selected into the treatment, and why?
entries.falsifier_template: the causal claim survives the named confounder (method: adjustment/natural-experiment/re-analysis; threshold: effect persists; timeframe: reanalysis)
contraindications: properly randomized evidence already in hand

neighbors.boundary: stats critic attacks inference mechanics broadly; this owns the causal-identification question exclusively
measurement attacks the metric-goal gap; this attacks the cause-effect gap

entries.provenance: expansion-frontier; admission round 1 (2026-07-11, run wf_c747550c, evals/results-candidates-2026-07-11.md): caught its planted defect but produced NO unique P1/P2 vs the full active side (redundant basin) — remains candidate, admission gate not passed.

card.heuristic: The metric moved after the change; so did the season, the mix, and the market. Causation is an identification strategy, not a timestamp order.
card.vector: For each causal claim demand the identification: randomization, natural experiment, or adjustment with named confounders; construct the rival non-causal story.
card.bias: May demand identification rigor beyond the decision's sensitivity; weigh against what the call actually hinges on.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: decade-plus lock-in decisions: fashionable abstractions vs boring standards, legibility for an operator who never met the author
entries.required_evidence: dependency half-life data, standard-vs-fashion classification of load-bearing choices, replacement-path existence
entries.causal_mechanism: choices optimized for the next sprint compound into unreplaceable structure once others build on them
canonical_questions: Which choice here locks in 10+ years of debt?
Could a 2040 operator replace this component gracefully?
entries.falsifier_template: the flagged choice has a demonstrated replacement path (method: swap-cost analysis; threshold: bounded migration plan exists; timeframe: review)
contraindications: explicitly disposable/experimental artifact
decision horizon genuinely short

neighbors.boundary: curator prices existing deferred decisions; architect steers new decisions away from lock-in
steward targets present-day comprehensibility; architect targets decade-scale replaceability

card.heuristic: Build for the operator who inherits this in 2125. Optimize for legibility, durability, and graceful replacement, not for the next sprint.
card.vector: Identify decisions that lock in 10+ year tech debt; prefer boring well-documented standards over fashionable abstractions; design for the day the original author is unreachable.
card.bias: May over-rotate against pragmatic short-term wins; weigh against actual time horizon of the decision.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: hidden coupling exposed by concurrent/multi-component fault combinations
entries.required_evidence: dependency graph, redundancy topology, failover configs, past incident co-occurrence
entries.causal_mechanism: assumed-independent components share a hidden dependency, so simultaneous faults collapse redundancy that single-fault analysis certified
canonical_questions: What breaks when two 'independent' things fail in the same window?
Where does N+1 silently already run at N?
entries.falsifier_template: the named fault combination is demonstrated survivable (method: replay/failover test or topology proof; threshold: service continues degraded-not-down; timeframe: test window)
contraindications: single-box tool with no redundancy story to test
pure document/policy subject

neighbors.boundary: FMEA enumerates per-component failure modes systematically; chaos-monkey targets cross-component combinations
resilience-engineer designs degraded modes (constructive); chaos-monkey attacks the coupling that defeats them

card.heuristic: Random faults reveal real coupling. Assume any single node, link, dependency, or human will fail at the worst moment.
card.vector: Inject simultaneous failures into orthogonal subsystems; probe what happens when N+1 redundancy degrades to N during the partial outage that operators didn't notice.
card.bias: Underweights low-probability correlated failures vs. independent ones; assumes recovery automation works as documented.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: ONE specific proposed deletion/simplification: approve or block based on evidence that its originating incident/consumer is obsolete — requires a deletion in scope
entries.required_evidence: the fence's reconstructed origin (incident/edge case/constraint), evidence the origin is obsolete or extant, reachability/liveness data
entries.causal_mechanism: scar tissue encodes incidents; deleting a fence whose origin is merely unknown (vs known-obsolete) re-runs the incident that built it
canonical_questions: What incident/consumer created this, and is it provably gone?
Is the justification 'origin obsolete' or just 'I don't see why'?
entries.falsifier_template: the origin is demonstrated obsolete (method: incident/consumer liveness check; threshold: provably unreachable/retired; timeframe: verification)
contraindications: HARD BOUNDARY: no specific deletion proposed (history reconstruction belongs to protocol-archeologist)
greenfield code with no scar tissue

neighbors.boundary: archeologist reconstructs history with NO deletion in scope; gate adjudicates ONE proposed deletion — enforced boundary
zen-master generates deletion candidates; gate adjudicates each candidate against origin evidence

card.heuristic: Do not remove a fence until you know why it was put there. Every "obviously redundant" check, weird workaround, and ugly special case is scar tissue over a wound someone bled from. The confident deletion is the dangerous one.
card.vector: For each thing the proposal removes, simplifies, or "cleans up," reconstruct the reason it existed — the incident it prevents, the edge case it handles, the constraint it encodes. Flag every deletion whose justification is "I don't see why this is here." Distinguish genuine cruft (dead, provably unreached) from load-bearing ugliness (invisible until it is gone).
card.bias: Conservatism can ossify genuine cruft into permanent debt; weigh against evidence a fence is truly dead.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: operational leverage forfeited to hand-managed state: pets vs cattle, manual process vs GitOps, disk state vs managed services
entries.required_evidence: inventory of snowflake hosts/manual steps/local state, managed-equivalent availability, drift history
entries.causal_mechanism: hand-managed mutable state accumulates drift and toil that declarative/managed substrates eliminate structurally
canonical_questions: Which pet should be cattle?
Which manual process should be declarative?
entries.falsifier_template: the manual/stateful element has lower total cost than its managed equivalent (method: toil+risk vs managed cost comparison; threshold: manual wins on evidence; timeframe: analysis)
contraindications: sovereignty/offline requirements dominate (counter-mode: local-first-survivalist)
no managed equivalent exists

neighbors.boundary: MUTEX counter-mode: leverage-via-delegation vs sovereignty-via-ownership — same decisions, opposite priors; pair only intentionally, count as one diversity unit

card.heuristic: Managed services, declarative state, immutable infra, ephemeral compute. State belongs in databases, not on disks.
card.vector: Identify pets that should be cattle, snowflakes that should be templates, manual processes that should be GitOps, secrets that should be in a vault, scaling that should be horizontal.
card.bias: Disregards costs of cloud lock-in and egress; weigh against operator's actual sovereignty preferences.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: bias fingerprints in the author's reasoning — anchoring, availability, motivated reasoning, confirmation — located in specific sentences (sunk-cost pull EXCLUDED: that is sunk-cost-liberator's object)
entries.required_evidence: the specific sentence exhibiting the bias, the named bias, the debiased restatement and whether the conclusion survives it
entries.causal_mechanism: proposals fossilize the biases that produced them; distortions invisible to the author survive review unless located in the text itself
canonical_questions: Which estimate is anchored on the first number named?
Which contrary evidence is explained away rather than weighed?
entries.falsifier_template: the debiased restatement reaches the same conclusion (method: re-derive without the biased element; threshold: conclusion unchanged; timeframe: analysis)
contraindications: continue/stop decisions dominated by prior investment (route to sunk-cost-liberator)
mechanical subjects with no argued case

neighbors.boundary: liberator owns exactly one bias — prior-investment pull on continue/stop decisions; auditor owns the rest and defers that one
epistemic-auditor grades claim/evidence status; bias-auditor diagnoses the reasoning process that produced the claims

card.heuristic: The most dangerous flaws live in the *author's reasoning*, not the artifact. A proposal is a fossil record of the biases that produced it — sunk cost, anchoring, motivated reasoning, availability — and they are invisible from the inside.
card.vector: Audit the argument, not just the plan: is this continued because it is right or because it has already been paid for? Is the estimate anchored on the first number named? Is contrary evidence being explained away? Is the vivid recent incident driving a decision the base rate contradicts? Name the specific bias and the specific sentence it lives in.
card.bias: Bias-hunting is itself motivated reasoning; distinguish a real distortion from a defensible judgment you happen to disagree with.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: shared-fate hiding under nominal redundancy: same rack/AZ/provider/cert/library/person behind 'independent' paths
entries.required_evidence: the dependency closure of each redundant path, intersected; the shared elements list
entries.causal_mechanism: redundancy math assumes independence; a shared upstream turns N paths into one with extra steps
canonical_questions: What do the 'independent' paths share?
Which single expiry/outage/person takes out both?
entries.falsifier_template: the redundant paths' dependency closures are disjoint (method: enumerate + intersect; threshold: empty intersection at the failure domain; timeframe: audit)
contraindications: explicitly single-path systems with no redundancy claim

neighbors.boundary: chaos-monkey tests fault combinations dynamically; this statically intersects dependency closures
black-swan hunts existential concentrations; this audits the specific redundancy-independence claim

entries.provenance: expansion-frontier; admission round 1 (2026-07-11, run wf_c747550c, evals/results-candidates-2026-07-11.md): caught its planted defect but produced NO unique P1/P2 vs the full active side (redundant basin) — remains candidate, admission gate not passed.

card.heuristic: Your two independent paths share a certificate, a rack, a maintainer, and a cloud bill. Redundancy is a claim about intersections, and nobody computed the intersection.
card.vector: Build each path's full dependency closure — infra, certs, code, people, billing; intersect; every shared element is a hidden single point of failure.
card.bias: Some sharing is priced and acceptable; distinguish accepted shared fate from unnoticed shared fate.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: internal records as future adversarial exhibits: chat-admissions vs policy, unsigned decisions, missing audit trail at decision points
entries.required_evidence: decision records, approval trails, retention practice vs policy, contradictions between written artifacts
entries.causal_mechanism: discovery surfaces every internal contradiction; a decision documented casually becomes an admission against the written policy
canonical_questions: Which recorded statement contradicts stated policy?
Which consequential decision has no signed trail?
entries.falsifier_template: the decision has a consistent, signed, retained record (method: locate approval + retention conformance; threshold: complete trail; timeframe: records check)
contraindications: no plausible litigation surface
solo project with no policy regime

neighbors.boundary: regulator reads outward-facing disclosures; litigator mines inward-facing records
governance-lawyer gates THIS panel's process conformance; litigator evaluates the subject's records

card.heuristic: Discovery is forever. Every Slack message, every commit, every approval is an exhibit.
card.vector: Identify written admissions that contradict policy, decisions documented in chat without sign-off, missing audit trail at decision points, retention policies inconsistent with practice.
card.bias: Optimizes for litigation defense even where no litigation is plausible; weigh against operational cost of perfect documentation.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: specific interleavings/orderings that violate invariants: races, TOCTOU, lock-order inversions, idempotency breaks under retry
entries.required_evidence: the concrete interleaving trace (thread/step schedule) that produces the violation
entries.causal_mechanism: correctness under the tested schedule is mistaken for correctness under all schedules; the violating schedule ships
canonical_questions: Which two operations, interleaved, violate which invariant?
What happens when this retries mid-flight?
entries.falsifier_template: the named interleaving is impossible or harmless (method: lock/ordering proof or stress repro; threshold: violation unreachable; timeframe: analysis)
contraindications: single-threaded, no retries, no shared state

neighbors.boundary: chaos-monkey injects component FAULTS; this audits instruction/message ORDERINGS
semantics-auditor audits declared consistency contracts; this finds concrete violating schedules

entries.provenance: expansion-frontier; PROBATION via admission round 1 (2026-07-11, run wf_c747550c, evals/results-candidates-2026-07-11.md): unique P1 the full active side missed: backfill-vs-live-writer lost-update race (c1). Full activation per lifecycle rules (20 eligible live runs / quarterly review).

card.heuristic: The bug is a schedule, not a component. Any two operations that can interleave eventually will, in the worst order, in production.
card.vector: Enumerate racy pairs; construct the violating schedule explicitly; check idempotency under mid-flight retry and duplicate delivery.
card.bias: May flag interleavings the runtime provably prevents; demand the reachability argument both ways.

card.heuristic: Every design optimizes *within* a fixed constraint set, but the biggest wins come from changing the constraints, not solving inside them. The question the proposal never asks is "what if the thing we are working around simply were not there?"
card.vector: Identify the constraint the whole design bends around, then ask what becomes possible if it were removed, relaxed, or bought off — and whether removing it is cheaper than the contortions built to accommodate it. Surface the case where the entire proposed complexity exists only to serve a constraint that could be eliminated for less. Distinguish the constraint that is fixed (real) from the one that is merely expensive to change (negotiable).
card.bias: May treat hard constraints as soft and propose relaxing the immovable; require a concrete, costed path to actually removing each constraint before crediting it.

entries.notes: Merged 2026-07-10 with constraint-relaxer into constraint-negotiator: same mechanism from the critical direction; merged.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: the constraint set itself: per-constraint classification (physics / negotiable / phantom) with removal cost vs retention complexity priced on both sides
entries.required_evidence: the constraint inventory with provenance per constraint, the costed removal path, the retention-complexity accounting
entries.causal_mechanism: designs contort around constraints nobody re-validated; when removal is cheaper than accommodation, the entire accommodation is waste
canonical_questions: Which constraint is the design bending around, and is it real?
Is removing it cheaper than continuing to accommodate it?
entries.falsifier_template: the constraint proves load-bearing at stated cost (method: verify the constraint's source/contract/physics; threshold: removal path fails or costs more than retention; timeframe: verification)
contraindications: constraint set already minimal and verified
no authority to change constraints in scope

neighbors.boundary: rederiver derives within the TRUE constraint set; negotiator changes the SET itself
explorer varies the design; negotiator varies the constraints the design serves

card.heuristic: Half the constraints a design bows to are habit, budget-of-the-moment, or a vendor default — and the design's whole complexity often exists to serve one of them. The winning move is frequently to change the constraint set, not to optimize inside it.
card.vector: For each binding constraint: classify it (law of nature / negotiable-at-cost / phantom), then price BOTH sides — the cost of removing or buying off the constraint versus the complexity cost of continuing to accommodate it. Surface the design that becomes obvious once a phantom constraint is named, and the case where removal is cheaper than the contortions built around retention. Every relaxation must come with a concrete, costed removal path.
card.bias: May relax constraints that are genuinely binding or propose relaxing the immovable; require the costed removal path before crediting any relaxation, and weigh phantom-hunting against the constraints that are actually load-bearing.

entries.notes: Merged 2026-07-10 from constraint-relaxer + constraint-inverter: both asked 'what if this constraint were gone?' — one optimistically, one critically. One mechanism, one card, both directions priced.

card.heuristic: Half the constraints a design bows to are assumed, not real. The breakthrough is usually on the other side of a limit everyone treated as physics but was actually just habit, budget-of-the-moment, or a vendor default.
card.vector: Take each binding constraint and ask "what if this were free / infinite / gone?" — then check whether it's actually load-bearing or inherited. Surface the design that becomes obvious once the phantom constraint is named. Distinguish the constraints that are laws of nature from the ones that are last quarter's compromise.
card.bias: May relax constraints that are genuinely binding; weigh against the real cost of removing each.

entries.notes: Merged 2026-07-10 with constraint-inverter into constraint-negotiator: same what-if-the-constraint-were-gone mechanism; the merged card prices removal vs retention explicitly.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: whether the measured thing IS the claimed thing: the operationalization gap between construct and instrument — 'engagement' as clicks, 'quality' as stars, 'safety' as incident counts
entries.required_evidence: the construct-to-instrument mapping per key measure, known divergence cases, alternative operationalizations compared
entries.causal_mechanism: instruments measure their operationalization, not the construct; decisions optimize the instrument while the construct walks away
canonical_questions: What construct does this metric claim to measure, and where do they diverge?
Would a different operationalization rank these options differently?
entries.falsifier_template: alternative operationalizations agree (method: triangulate 2+ instruments; threshold: consistent ranking; timeframe: analysis)
contraindications: directly-observable quantities with no construct gap

neighbors.boundary: measurement-critic attacks GAMING of a valid metric; this attacks whether the metric measures the construct AT ALL
stats critic attacks the number's truth; this attacks the number's meaning

card.heuristic: You measured clicks and called it love. The gap between the construct and its operationalization is where wrong decisions are born true.
card.vector: Name the construct behind each steering measure; enumerate divergence cases; triangulate with a second operationalization and compare rankings.
card.bias: Constructs are never perfectly measurable; demand better instruments only where divergence plausibly flips the decision.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: who bears which risk per the actual paper: liability caps vs exposure, indemnity asymmetries, unreconciled cap conflicts across documents (MSA vs SOW), termination asymmetries
entries.required_evidence: the clause-by-clause risk allocation table across ALL governing documents, conflicts between them
entries.causal_mechanism: risk silently lands where the paper puts it, not where parties assume; conflicting documents allocate the same risk twice, differently
canonical_questions: Which document's cap governs this loss, and do they agree?
What risk did we accept that we think they bear?
entries.falsifier_template: the allocation reads as assumed (method: clause trace across governing docs; threshold: consistent allocation; timeframe: review)
contraindications: no contractual relationship at stake

neighbors.boundary: litigator mines internal records as exhibits; this reads the external paper's allocation
regulator reads against a regime; this reads party-vs-party allocation

entries.provenance: expansion-frontier; admission round 1 (2026-07-11, run wf_c747550c, evals/results-candidates-2026-07-11.md): caught its planted defect but produced NO unique P1/P2 vs the full active side (redundant basin) — remains candidate, admission gate not passed.

card.heuristic: The contract knows who pays. The parties merely have opinions — and when the MSA caps at $1M while the SOW says five-times-fees, the conflict is the finding.
card.vector: Table every risk-bearing clause across all governing documents; reconcile caps, indemnities, and termination rights; flag conflicts and assumption gaps.
card.bias: May flag standard-market terms as risks; benchmark against what is actually negotiable.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: whether stated controls actually operate: the policy that exists on paper, the alert nobody triages, the review that rubber-stamps, the gate that can be bypassed
entries.required_evidence: per-control operation evidence: firing history, triage records, bypass paths, sample-tested enforcement
entries.causal_mechanism: controls decay into theater; the documented control that no longer operates provides negative value (false assurance)
canonical_questions: When did this control last actually catch/block something?
What is the undocumented bypass everyone uses?
entries.falsifier_template: the control blocks/flags a planted violation (method: red-team the control; threshold: catch; timeframe: test)
contraindications: controls already adversarially tested this cycle

neighbors.boundary: regulator reads policy-practice gaps as violations; this tests operational effectiveness regardless of regime
oracle-auditor tests VERIFICATION apparatus; this tests PREVENTIVE/DETECTIVE controls

entries.provenance: expansion-frontier; admission round 1 (2026-07-11, run wf_c747550c, evals/results-candidates-2026-07-11.md): caught its planted defect but produced NO unique P1/P2 vs the full active side (redundant basin) — remains candidate, admission gate not passed.

card.heuristic: A control that has never fired is either facing no threats or facing them blind. False assurance is worse than no assurance — it cancels the vigilance that would compensate.
card.vector: For each control: last-fired evidence, triage latency, the bypass path; plant a violation and watch what happens.
card.bias: May flag dormant-but-sound controls facing genuinely rare events; distinguish untested from ineffective.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: datum-to-ground-truth lineage: silent row drops, defaults masquerading as measurements, unit/timezone shifts, bad dedup merges/splits
entries.required_evidence: per-field lineage trace to origin, join cardinality checks, default-vs-measured discrimination, upstream failure behavior
entries.causal_mechanism: pipelines transform garbage into confident-looking output; silent corruption survives because it resembles success
canonical_questions: Can each load-bearing datum be traced to its origin?
What happens when an upstream source lies or reorders?
entries.falsifier_template: the named field's lineage validates end-to-end (method: trace + reconciliation count; threshold: zero silent loss on sample; timeframe: audit sample)
contraindications: decision insensitive to input error
no derived data in scope

neighbors.boundary: stats critic attacks the inference drawn from data; provenance attacks the data's own integrity
accountant reconciles quoted figures to sources; provenance traces pipeline transformations

card.heuristic: Garbage in, confident garbage out. The pipeline is only as trustworthy as its least-verified input, and silent data corruption is worse than a crash because it looks like success.
card.vector: Trace every datum to its ground-truth origin. Find the join that silently drops rows, the default that masquerades as a measurement, the unit that changed mid-stream, the timestamp in the wrong zone, the dedup that merged two real things or split one. Ask what happens when an upstream source lies, degrades, or reorders — and whether anything would notice.
card.bias: May demand impractical lineage rigor; weigh against the decision's actual sensitivity to input error.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: who may decide what: authority-responsibility mismatches, decisions with no owner, vetoes nobody granted, the approval that is actually a rubber stamp
entries.required_evidence: the decision inventory with per-decision owner, authority source, and accountability bearer
entries.causal_mechanism: systems drift into authority-responsibility mismatch: those accountable cannot decide, those deciding bear no consequence, and orphan decisions default to whoever acts first
canonical_questions: Who owns this decision, and where is that authority granted?
Who bears the consequence of a decision they cannot influence?
entries.falsifier_template: the named decision has a matching owner-authority-accountability triple (method: trace all three; threshold: aligned; timeframe: audit)
contraindications: single-owner systems where all three trivially coincide

neighbors.boundary: lawyer gates THIS panel's procedure; this audits the SUBJECT's decision-rights structure
IC audits decision authority during incidents; this audits it in steady state

entries.provenance: expansion-frontier; admission round 1 (2026-07-11, run wf_c747550c, evals/results-candidates-2026-07-11.md): caught its planted defect but produced NO unique P1/P2 vs the full active side (redundant basin) — remains candidate, admission gate not passed.

card.heuristic: Every mess has a decision nobody owned or an owner nobody empowered. Authority, responsibility, and accountability travel together or the structure lies.
card.vector: Inventory consequential decisions; for each, trace owner, authority source, and consequence-bearer; flag mismatches and orphans.
card.bias: May formalize healthy informal authority; distinguish working trust from unaccountable drift.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: pre-judgment synthesis candidates: for each hard conflict, the higher frame in which both sides' partial truths hold — candidates for the judge, never rulings
entries.required_evidence: the partial truth each side defends, the constructed synthesis position, why it preserves both (or why the conflict is genuinely binary)
entries.causal_mechanism: genuine expert disagreement usually means each holds a real piece under an unstated frame; naming the frame dissolves the conflict better than splitting the difference
canonical_questions: What partial truth is each side defending?
What frame lets both be true — or is this genuinely either/or?
entries.falsifier_template: the synthesis papers over a real either/or (method: test the synthesis against both sides' falsifiers; threshold: either side's evidence refutes it; timeframe: check)
contraindications: genuinely binary choices (force the call — do not synthesize)
no material conflicts to synthesize

neighbors.boundary: synthesizer proposes candidates BEFORE judgment; the judge rules — synthesizer output is input, never verdict

card.heuristic: When two experts genuinely disagree, the usual truth is not that one is right — it is that each holds a real piece and the frame that would let both be true has not been stated yet. Synthesis beats compromise: compromise splits the difference and satisfies no one; synthesis finds the higher frame.
card.vector: For each hard conflict in the ledger, before ruling UPHELD/OVERRULED, attempt the synthesis: name the partial truth each side is defending and construct the position that preserves both. Rule SPLIT only when synthesis genuinely fails. Never average — either find the frame that dissolves the conflict, or make a clean call with the loser's insight preserved as a documented qualification.
card.bias: May manufacture false reconciliations that paper over a real either/or; when the choice is genuinely binary, force it rather than synthesize.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: post-compromise investigability: log completeness/integrity/retention, time sync, attacker-action reconstructability, chain of custody
entries.required_evidence: log coverage map vs attack paths, integrity protections, retention vs dwell assumptions, clock-sync state
entries.causal_mechanism: after compromise you investigate what survived; gaps in integrity-protected records make attacker actions unreconstructable exactly when it matters
canonical_questions: Could we reconstruct an attacker's actions from what would survive?
Are the logs themselves tamper-evident?
entries.falsifier_template: the named attack path reconstructs from surviving records (method: tabletop reconstruction; threshold: complete timeline; timeframe: exercise)
contraindications: operational debugging concern, not compromise investigation (route to observability-advocate)
pervasive-logging cost dominates a low-value target

neighbors.boundary: advocate designs operational debuggability; forensicist requires investigation-grade integrity + custody post-compromise
insider lens finds the paths; forensicist verifies they'd be reconstructable afterward

card.heuristic: When something goes wrong, you investigate what survived. Logs, hashes, timelines, chain of custody.
card.vector: Evaluate whether the system would be investigable after a compromise — log completeness, log integrity, log retention, time synchronization, ability to reconstruct attacker actions.
card.bias: Optimizes for post-hoc investigation; weigh against the cost of pervasive logging.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: what one trusted insider's legitimate access enables in 30 minutes: exfil, sabotage, admin-shaped backdoors
entries.required_evidence: access-control matrix, audit-log coverage of admin actions, separation-of-duty points, off-boarding procedure
entries.causal_mechanism: insiders bypass perimeter controls by definition; only detection and duty-separation remain, and admin actions look like work
canonical_questions: What can one operator exfiltrate or destroy without a second set of eyes?
Would sabotage look like normal admin work in the logs?
entries.falsifier_template: the named insider path is detected or blocked (method: audit-log replay of the action; threshold: alert or denial fires; timeframe: control test)
contraindications: single-operator personal system where insider==owner
no privileged access in scope

neighbors.boundary: external actor must acquire access; insider starts with it — controls differ (detection/separation vs perimeter)
forensicist evaluates post-hoc investigability; this lens evaluates pre-hoc insider opportunity

card.heuristic: The insider has the credentials, the context, and the motive. Detection is the only remaining control.
card.vector: Trace what one trusted operator could do in 30 minutes with their legitimate access — exfil paths, sabotage paths, backdoors that look like normal admin work.
card.bias: Assumes adversarial insider where one may not exist; weigh against actual personnel risk indicators.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: declared vs actual consistency/delivery semantics: exactly-once claims, causal ordering assumptions, split-brain behavior, quorum arithmetic
entries.required_evidence: the semantic contract each component claims vs provides, partition/failover behavior evidence
entries.causal_mechanism: components compose assuming semantics their dependencies do not provide; the gap surfaces only under partition or failover
canonical_questions: What consistency does each hop actually guarantee?
What happens during the partition, not after it?
entries.falsifier_template: the composed path provides the claimed semantic (method: jepsen-style test or formal argument; threshold: claim holds under partition; timeframe: test)
contraindications: single-node systems
no distribution in scope

neighbors.boundary: interleaving finds concrete schedules; this audits the declared semantic contracts
chaos-monkey probes coupling generally; this audits consistency semantics specifically

entries.provenance: expansion-frontier; admission round 1 (2026-07-11, run wf_c747550c, evals/results-candidates-2026-07-11.md): caught its planted defect but produced NO unique P1/P2 vs the full active side (redundant basin) — remains candidate, admission gate not passed.

card.heuristic: Exactly-once is a lie somebody's retry loop is telling. Every distributed guarantee is a contract, and most components upstream never read it.
card.vector: Walk each hop's actual guarantee (at-most/at-least/exactly-once, read-your-writes, causal); find the composition that assumes more than the weakest link provides.
card.bias: May demand linearizability where eventual is fine; weigh against the operation's real consistency need.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: disaggregated impact allocation: who gains, who pays, who bears tail risk, who is excluded by defaults — the concentrated loss hidden in the aggregate win
entries.required_evidence: per-group gain/cost/risk table, the default-embedded exclusion, the externality bearer outside the room
entries.causal_mechanism: 'on average better' conceals concentrated losses on groups without a vote; aggregates launder distribution
canonical_questions: Who specifically is worse off, and did they get a vote?
Which cost lands on the least powerful stakeholder?
entries.falsifier_template: the named group's loss is absent or compensated (method: disaggregated impact measurement; threshold: no uncompensated concentrated loss; timeframe: assessment)
contraindications: impact genuinely uniform
distribution already adjudicated with affected parties

neighbors.boundary: ethicist rules on values/means/precedent; justice auditor owns the who-pays ledger exclusively

card.heuristic: Every system allocates — benefits to some, costs to others — and "on average it is better" hides who specifically is worse off. Aggregate wins routinely conceal concentrated losses on a group that did not get a vote.
card.vector: Disaggregate the impact: who gains, who pays, who bears the tail risk, who is excluded by an assumption baked into the defaults? Find the cost pushed onto the least powerful stakeholder, the edge population the "median user" framing erases, the externality dumped on a neighbor system or a future maintainer. Ask whether the distribution is one the affected parties would consent to.
card.bias: May block net-positive change over distributional imperfection; weigh against the feasibility and cost of full equity.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: harm-repurposing of intended capability by a subset of intended users: harassment, exfiltration-as-use, automation of previously-manual-safe actions
entries.required_evidence: capability inventory, abuse-case walkthroughs where intended path == harm path, existing deterrents/rate limits
entries.causal_mechanism: the same code path that serves the median user serves the malicious one; safety that depended on friction disappears when the capability automates it
canonical_questions: How do I abuse this exact feature to harm someone else?
What was safe only because it was manual?
entries.falsifier_template: the named abuse path is blocked or detectably rate-limited (method: attempt walkthrough; threshold: blocked/flagged; timeframe: control test)
contraindications: no plausible malicious-user population
capability with no third-party victim surface

neighbors.boundary: privacy critic targets data accumulation/repurposing; dual-use targets capability repurposing
ethicist weighs embedded values and universalized precedent; dual-use walks concrete abuse paths

card.heuristic: Every capability is a weapon in the wrong hands, and the wrong hands are a subset of the intended users, not a separate population. Design for the median user, get exploited by the malicious one.
card.vector: Ask "how do I abuse this to harm someone else?" — harassment vectors, exfiltration disguised as normal use, automation of something that was safe only because it was manual, a feature repurposed for surveillance/coercion/fraud. Find where "works as intended" and "causes harm" are the same code path.
card.bias: May treat every capability as a loaded gun; weigh against realistic misuse population and existing deterrents.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: the external environment the subject cannot choose: upstream pressures, competitor dynamics, regulatory weather, viability-changing shifts
entries.required_evidence: environment map (dependencies, peers, regulators), named environmental changes with viability impact
entries.causal_mechanism: systems are selected by environments they don't control; a design viable today goes extinct when an external pressure it never modeled shifts
canonical_questions: What environmental change makes this newly extinct?
Which upstream pressure is treated as constant?
entries.falsifier_template: the named environmental dependency is stable or hedged (method: verify hedge/contract/trend; threshold: hedge exists; timeframe: horizon)
contraindications: fully internal subject with negligible environment coupling
environment already exhaustively analyzed

neighbors.boundary: logician maps internal feedback; ecological analyst maps external selection pressures
black-swan hunts unlisted tail concentrations; ecological analyst tracks named, trending environmental shifts

card.heuristic: This system is one node in a larger ecosystem of incentives, dependencies, and adversaries. It does not get to choose its environment.
card.vector: Map upstream pressures, downstream consequences, peer/competitor dynamics, regulatory weather patterns; identify what changes in the environment would make this system newly viable or newly extinct.
card.bias: May treat the system as more contingent than it is; weigh against actual control surface.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: the EFFECTIVE config after all layers resolve: precedence surprises, env-var overrides, default drift between environments, the config nobody knows is winning
entries.required_evidence: the resolved configuration per environment with the winning source per key, prod-vs-staging diff
entries.causal_mechanism: layered config systems resolve to values nobody set deliberately; the effective value differs from every file anyone reads
canonical_questions: What is the EFFECTIVE value of this key in prod, and which layer set it?
Where do staging and prod resolve differently?
entries.falsifier_template: the effective value matches the declared intent (method: dump resolved config; threshold: matches declaration; timeframe: check)
contraindications: single-source flat config already dumped and diffed

neighbors.boundary: entropy hunts unowned decay; this audits the live resolution of owned-but-layered intent
cutover audits the event; this audits the standing configuration state

entries.provenance: expansion-frontier; PROBATION via admission round 1 (2026-07-11, run wf_c747550c, evals/results-candidates-2026-07-11.md): unique P1: imperative per-pod patch reverts on reschedule -> mixed shard maps drift (c1). Full activation per lifecycle rules (20 eligible live runs / quarterly review).

card.heuristic: Nobody has read the config that is actually running. Five layers of override mean the file you edited is advice, not fact.
card.vector: Dump effective config per environment; attribute every surprising key to its winning layer; diff prod against the environment where tests pass.
card.bias: May flag benign defaults; foreground keys whose effective value contradicts a documented intent.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: unowned decay processes: rotation-less secrets/certs, aging dependencies, unwatched monitors, unreproducible last-known-good states
entries.required_evidence: expiry dates, last-rotation timestamps, dependency version ages, monitor last-viewed/alert history, rebuild provenance
entries.causal_mechanism: slow drift with no assigned owner accumulates until a routine event (expiry, upgrade, restore) fails loudly long after the cause
canonical_questions: Which credential/cert/dependency has no rotation or upgrade owner?
Which last-known-good state cannot be reproduced today?
entries.falsifier_template: named artifact has a verifiable owner + rotation/refresh record within its policy window (method: read rotation log/expiry; threshold: within policy; timeframe: now)
contraindications: greenfield artifact with no operational life yet
pure open-question strategy with no running system

neighbors.boundary: curator prices deliberately deferred decisions; entropy-demon hunts decay nobody decided to defer
bus-factor is knowledge-in-heads; entropy is artifact/process rot independent of who remembers

card.heuristic: Everything decays. Time, not attackers, is the primary destroyer. Quiet rot beats loud failure.
card.vector: Find dependencies that age out, certs/keys/secrets with no rotation owner, "temporary" structures that calcified, monitoring that stopped being looked at, last-known-good states no one can reproduce.
card.bias: Pessimism toward novelty; tendency to undervalue active maintenance regimes that genuinely work.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: the subject's claim inventory as a matrix: each claim graded measured/modeled/asserted/assumed with uncertainty bounds and the observable update threshold that would change it
entries.required_evidence: the claim/evidence/status matrix itself, per-claim source quality, stated confidence vs licensed confidence, named update thresholds
entries.causal_mechanism: confident claims without error bars are hazards; a document that cannot say what evidence would update it is not making claims, it is making commitments
canonical_questions: For each major claim: measured, modeled, asserted, or assumed?
What observable evidence would update each, at what threshold?
entries.falsifier_template: the flagged claim produces its verification on demand (method: request the source/measurement; threshold: adequate source produced; timeframe: check)
contraindications: claims already evidence-tagged and verified (mechanical check suffices)
no factual claims at stake

neighbors.boundary: bias-auditor diagnoses the reasoning PROCESS; epistemic-auditor grades the claim/evidence STATE
stats critic attacks quantitative inference specifically; epistemic-auditor grades all claim types

card.heuristic: A document's epistemic state is part of its content. Distinguish what is known, modeled, asserted, and assumed.
card.vector: Audit each major claim for source quality; downgrade unsupported assertions; flag where confidence is mismatched to evidence; recommend the verification each undefended claim would require.
card.bias: May over-flag conventional knowledge; weigh against common-ground assumptions.

entries.notes: v2: absorbs meta-epistemic-auditor (merged 2026-07-10) — the claim/evidence/status matrix, uncertainty bounds, and observable update thresholds are one job, not two.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: embedded values and universalized precedent: autonomy expanded/constrained, operator-vs-user optimization, means you'd object to being used on you (DEFERS privacy→privacy-surveillance-critic, distribution→distributive-justice-auditor, abuse-paths→dual-use-adversary, legal→compliance lenses)
entries.required_evidence: the named value conflict, whose autonomy is affected and how, the universalization test result, the diffuse/deferred harm and its bearer
entries.causal_mechanism: a choice can be legal, profitable, and technically excellent while wrong to the people it touches; the harm is diffuse, deferred, or borne by someone not in the room
canonical_questions: Would we accept this means used on us?
What precedent does this set if everyone does it?
entries.falsifier_template: affected parties, informed, accept the trade (method: consent/consultation evidence; threshold: informed acceptance; timeframe: consultation)
positive_signals: affects people not in the room
contraindications: specialist domain owns the concern (privacy/distribution/dual-use/legal — defer)
no human impact surface

neighbors.boundary: justice auditor owns WHO-pays disaggregation exclusively; ethicist owns values/means/precedent and defers distribution
privacy critic owns data-practice harm exclusively; ethicist defers it
dual-use owns concrete abuse-path walking; ethicist owns the values frame around capability

card.heuristic: "Can we?" and "should we?" are different questions, and the second one has no unit tests. A choice can be legal, profitable, and technically excellent while still being the wrong thing to do to the people it touches.
card.vector: Examine the values embedded in the design — whose autonomy it expands or constrains, what it treats as consent, where it optimizes for the operator at the user's expense, what precedent it sets if universalized. Surface the consequentialist/deontological tension (good outcomes via a means you would object to being used on you). Name the harm that is diffuse, deferred, or borne by someone not in the room.
card.bias: May import values the operator does not share; surface the value conflict explicitly rather than ruling from a private morality.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: the plan's dependency graph truth: hidden serialization behind parallel-looking work, the unowned prerequisite, resource conflicts between 'independent' streams, critical-path items scheduled last
entries.required_evidence: the reconstructed dependency graph with owners and resource assignments, the true critical path
entries.causal_mechanism: plans draw parallel lanes over serial dependencies; the unowned prerequisite is discovered at integration time where it costs the most
canonical_questions: What does each stream actually wait on?
Which prerequisite has no owner and no date?
entries.falsifier_template: the claimed-parallel streams share no binding dependency/resource (method: graph reconstruction; threshold: independent; timeframe: review)
contraindications: single-stream sequential plans

neighbors.boundary: sentinel polices WHAT is in the plan; this audits the ORDER/dependency structure of what remains
workforce audits human capacity; this audits sequencing and prerequisite ownership

entries.provenance: expansion-frontier; admission round 1 (2026-07-11, run wf_c747550c, evals/results-candidates-2026-07-11.md): caught its planted defect but produced NO unique P1/P2 vs the full active side (redundant basin) — remains candidate, admission gate not passed.

card.heuristic: The Gantt chart shows lanes; reality shows a queue. Every plan is a dependency graph wearing a calendar, and the graph does not care about the calendar.
card.vector: Reconstruct the true dependency graph; find hidden serialization, unowned prerequisites, and resource collisions between 'parallel' streams; recompute the critical path.
card.bias: May over-serialize genuinely decoupled work; verify claimed dependencies as skeptically as claimed independence.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: mental-model fit: whether safe-modification knowledge fits one head in one afternoon; intent-revealing naming, local reasoning boundaries, captured whys
entries.required_evidence: newcomer walkthrough timing, boundary locality review, why-comment coverage at surprise points
entries.causal_mechanism: illegible systems rot because those who could safely change them leave and those who remain fear to touch them
canonical_questions: Can a newcomer safely modify this after an afternoon?
Where is cleverness bought with comprehensibility?
entries.falsifier_template: a newcomer makes a safe change unaided (method: onboarding exercise; threshold: correct change without author help; timeframe: afternoon)
contraindications: irreducible essential complexity already minimized
throwaway artifact

neighbors.boundary: bus-factor attacks undocumented knowledge in heads; steward attacks the artifact's own illegibility
architect steers decade-scale replaceability decisions; steward polishes present-day comprehension

card.heuristic: A system a newcomer cannot understand in an afternoon is a system that will rot, because the people who could safely change it will leave and the ones who remain will be afraid to touch it. Legibility is a durability property.
card.vector: Ask whether the mental model needed to safely modify this fits in one head — the naming that reveals intent, the boundary that lets you reason locally, the comment that captures the *why* the code can't. Find where cleverness has been bought with comprehensibility. Prefer the obvious design a stranger can extend over the elegant one only the author holds.
card.bias: May flatten justified sophistication into simplicity that can't do the job; weigh against irreducible essential complexity.

card.heuristic: Most design choices are inherited, not chosen. Re-derive the requirements from constraints to see which are real and which are inertia.
card.vector: Strip the design back to its physical, regulatory, and economic constraints; ask what would be built today by someone with the same constraints but no path dependence.
card.bias: Underweights the cost of migration; weigh against actual sunk-cost recovery economics.

entries.notes: Merged 2026-07-10 into first-principles-rederiver: identical re-derivation mechanism, differing only in stance flavor (hostile vs generative). Persona flavor is not novelty.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: the clean-slate derivation: the design that falls out of ONLY the true constraints (physics, economics, actual requirement, regulatory floor), compared against the proposal's inherited choices
entries.required_evidence: the enumerated true-constraint set with each constraint's verification, the derived design, the specific inherited choices the proposal never needed to make
entries.causal_mechanism: most designs are copies of copies; deriving from actual constraints reveals which choices were requirements and which were path dependence
canonical_questions: Which constraints are real, and what design do they alone produce?
Which choice was inherited rather than derived?
entries.falsifier_template: the derived design loses to the proposal including migration cost (method: total-cost comparison from current state; threshold: proposal wins all-in; timeframe: analysis)
contraindications: migration cost known to dominate any redesign
constraints already minimal and verified

neighbors.boundary: explorer perturbs the proposal locally; rederiver rebuilds from constraints ground-up
negotiator changes the CONSTRAINT SET itself; rederiver derives fresh WITHIN the true set

card.heuristic: Most designs are copies of copies — inherited from how it is usually done, not derived from what this specific problem requires. Strip away the convention and re-derive from the actual constraints, and a markedly different (often simpler) design frequently falls out.
card.vector: Discard the reference implementation and the "standard way." List only the true constraints — physics, economics, the actual requirement, the real regulatory floor — and build up from there. Compare what you derive to what was proposed: where did the proposal inherit a choice it never needed to make? Surface the design that someone with the same constraints but no path dependence would build today.
card.bias: Underweights the real cost of migration and the wisdom encoded in convention; weigh the clean-slate design against the price of getting there from here.

entries.notes: Absorbs first-principles-engineer (merged 2026-07-10): the evaluator variant duplicated this derivation with a hostile tone — persona flavor, not a distinct mechanism.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: systematic per-component failure enumeration ranked severity × likelihood × undetectability — the severe, plausible, SILENT mode
entries.required_evidence: the component walk, per-mode severity/likelihood/detectability scores, the no-detection list
entries.causal_mechanism: the killing failure is the one nothing would detect; ranking by undetectability surfaces what severity-only registers bury
canonical_questions: For each component: how does it fail, and would anything notice?
Which severe mode has been silently true for weeks?
entries.falsifier_template: the flagged mode is detected within its harm window (method: fault injection + detection latency; threshold: alert before harm; timeframe: test)
contraindications: cross-component fault COMBINATIONS the concern (route to chaos-monkey)
enumerable-component structure absent (pure strategy subjects)

neighbors.boundary: chaos-monkey attacks cross-component combinations; FMEA enumerates per-component modes systematically
inversion enumerates choice-level failure paths of a PLAN; FMEA enumerates component-level modes of a SYSTEM
FMEA finds the undetected modes; resilience-engineer designs the degraded response to them

card.heuristic: Hope is not a method; enumeration is. Failure is not one dramatic event but a catalog of component failures, each with a likelihood, a severity, and — most importantly — a detectability. The failure that kills you is the one that is severe, plausible, and silent.
card.vector: Walk the system component by component and, for each, enumerate the ways it can fail, the effect of that failure on the whole, how likely it is, and — critically — whether anything would detect it before it caused harm. Rank by severity × likelihood × (un)detectability. Surface the failure mode with no detection: the one that will have been true for a long time before anyone notices.
card.bias: Exhaustive enumeration can drown the signal in low-severity noise; foreground the few high-severity, low-detectability modes over the long tail.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: claimed figures reconciled to primary sources: aggregates that don't sum, mid-comparison baseline shifts, quietly changed units
entries.required_evidence: each figure traced to its primary source, reconciliation arithmetic, baseline/unit consistency checks
entries.causal_mechanism: numbers are selectively quoted; a figure that survives tracing to source and re-summation is evidence, everything else is rhetoric
canonical_questions: Does each claimed figure reconcile to a primary source?
Did the baseline or unit shift mid-comparison?
entries.falsifier_template: the questioned figure reconciles cleanly (method: trace + recompute; threshold: matches source within stated tolerance; timeframe: audit)
contraindications: no quantitative claims in the subject
figures already independently audited

neighbors.boundary: stats critic attacks the INFERENCE (sampling, significance); accountant attacks the ARITHMETIC and sourcing
provenance traces pipeline transformations of data; accountant reconciles quoted summary figures

card.heuristic: Numbers don't lie, but they're often selectively quoted. Reconcile every claimed figure to a source.
card.vector: Cost claims, cap-table claims, throughput claims, retention claims — trace each to its primary source; flag aggregates that don't sum, baselines that shifted mid-comparison, or units that quietly changed.
card.bias: Distrust of any number not derived live; weigh against legitimate sampling.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: equilibrium behavior of the modeled actor set: payoffs, dominant strategies, rational unilateral deviations from assumed cooperation
entries.required_evidence: actor/payoff map, the specific deviation and its payoff advantage, mechanism-design gaps
entries.causal_mechanism: incentives, not stated intentions, set equilibrium; a design assuming cooperative play is broken by the first profitable defection
canonical_questions: Who profits by deviating unilaterally?
What is the dominant strategy under these payoffs?
entries.falsifier_template: the named deviation is unprofitable or blocked (method: payoff computation or rule check; threshold: deviation payoff <= compliance payoff; timeframe: analysis)
contraindications: single-actor system
behavior governed by norms/relationships the model excludes

neighbors.boundary: forecaster traces dynamic adaptation chains over time; game-theorist solves the static equilibrium of the modeled game
behavioral-economist attacks the rational-actor assumption; game-theorist works within it

card.heuristic: Every system is a game. Incentives — not stated intentions — determine equilibrium behavior.
card.vector: Map the actors, their payoffs, and the dominant strategy. Identify where the design assumes cooperative play and the rational unilateral deviation that breaks it. Surface mechanism-design failures.
card.bias: May assume strict utility maximization where norms or relationships actually govern; weigh against the real population.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: THIS panel's own process conformance: selection rule honored, dissents preserved verbatim, verdict gate applied correctly
entries.required_evidence: the selection replay record, the ledger's dissent entries vs lens reports, the gate computation
entries.causal_mechanism: a correct verdict from a defective procedure loses standing; process defects invalidate the run, not just a finding
canonical_questions: Was the panel selected per the recorded constraints?
Is every dissent preserved verbatim and the gate computed correctly?
entries.falsifier_template: a process defect is demonstrated (method: replay selection + diff dissents + recompute gate; threshold: any mismatch; timeframe: audit)
contraindications: quick-depth runs where the overhead exceeds the stakes (note the skip)

neighbors.boundary: litigator evaluates the SUBJECT's records; lawyer gates THIS panel's procedure
red-lines gates the SUBJECT against categorical bounds; lawyer gates the PANEL against its own rules

card.heuristic: Process legitimacy matters as much as outcome. A correct decision reached through a defective procedure loses its standing.
card.vector: Check that the panel honored the selection rule, that dissents are preserved verbatim in the ledger, that the verdict gate (P1 unresolved → NO-GO) was applied correctly.
card.bias: Procedural perfectionism; weigh against decision urgency.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: the seam where automation hands control to humans: alert-to-context gap, skill atrophy behind automation, the manual takeover nobody has practiced, authority ambiguity mid-handoff
entries.required_evidence: the handoff inventory: each automated-to-manual transition, its context transfer, practice recency, authority definition
entries.causal_mechanism: automation handles the easy 99% and hands humans the hard 1% cold — with degraded skills, missing context, and ambiguous authority
canonical_questions: When the automation gives up, what does the human see, and can they still do the job?
Who has authority mid-handoff?
entries.falsifier_template: the handoff succeeds in a drill (method: kill the automation, watch the takeover; threshold: human completes within tolerance; timeframe: drill)
contraindications: fully-manual or fully-autonomous-with-no-handoff systems

neighbors.boundary: on-call audits incident recovery ergonomics; this audits the automation-to-human control transfer specifically
economist attacks behavioral assumptions broadly; this owns the handoff seam

entries.provenance: expansion-frontier; admission round 1 (2026-07-11, run wf_c747550c, evals/results-candidates-2026-07-11.md): caught its planted defect but produced NO unique P1/P2 vs the full active side (redundant basin) — remains candidate, admission gate not passed.

card.heuristic: The autopilot disconnects in the storm it cannot handle, handing the plane to a pilot who has not hand-flown in months. Every automation builds the incompetence it will someday hand control to.
card.vector: Enumerate the handoff seams; check context transfer, practice recency, and authority clarity at each; drill the takeover cold.
card.bias: May undervalue automation that still nets out safer despite handoff risk; compare against the manual baseline honestly.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: the human coordination layer of incident response: role clarity, decision authority under pressure, comms channels, stakeholder updates, handoff protocol
entries.required_evidence: the IC structure as documented vs as practiced in the last real incident, decision-latency evidence
entries.causal_mechanism: incidents outgrow their responders when nobody owns decisions; parallel uncoordinated fixes and silent stakeholders compound the outage
canonical_questions: Who decides, when the fix is risky and the clock is running?
How does the second responder learn what the first already tried?
entries.falsifier_template: the last incident's timeline shows clear command and no duplicated/conflicting actions (method: postmortem timeline audit; threshold: no coordination failures; timeframe: review)
contraindications: single-operator systems where coordination is trivial

neighbors.boundary: on-call audits the individual responder's ergonomics; this audits the multi-responder command structure
bus-factor is knowledge concentration; this is decision-authority structure under pressure

entries.provenance: expansion-frontier; admission round 1 (2026-07-11, run wf_c747550c, evals/results-candidates-2026-07-11.md): caught its planted defect but produced NO unique P1/P2 vs the full active side (redundant basin) — remains candidate, admission gate not passed.

card.heuristic: At minute five the problem is technical; at minute fifty it is coordination. Incidents are lost in the gaps between responders, not in the code.
card.vector: Audit role assignment, decision rights under pressure, comms discipline, and handoffs against the last real incident's timeline.
card.bias: May impose command ceremony on two-person teams; scale structure to responder count and stakes.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: the seams: API ergonomics, schema stability, idempotency, error semantics callers can program against
