entries.required_evidence: contract review from the caller's side, versioning policy, idempotency-key presence, webhook delivery guarantees
entries.causal_mechanism: value concentrates at integration seams; an excellent component with a hostile contract loses to a mediocre one with a clean contract
canonical_questions: Can a caller program against these error semantics?
What breaks integrators on the next schema change?
entries.falsifier_template: a naive integrator completes the named flow from docs alone (method: integration walkthrough; threshold: success without maintainer help; timeframe: session)
contraindications: no integration surface
single-consumer internal code

neighbors.boundary: polisher covers human-facing surface; weaver covers machine-facing contracts

card.heuristic: Systems exist to be connected. The value is in the seams. A great component nobody can integrate with is a worse outcome than a mediocre one with a clean contract.
card.vector: Evaluate API ergonomics, schema stability, webhook reliability, idempotency keys, error semantics that callers can actually program against.
card.bias: May privilege external API ergonomics over internal simplicity; weigh against caller universe.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: the invariants the design claims (or implies) it maintains: stated where? checked where? violated-detected how?
entries.required_evidence: the enumerated invariant list with, per invariant, its statement location, enforcement point, and violation-detection path
entries.causal_mechanism: designs maintain invariants nobody wrote down; unstated invariants get broken by correct-looking changes and detected by nothing
canonical_questions: What must always be true here, and where is that written?
Which invariant has no enforcement point?
entries.falsifier_template: the named invariant is stated, enforced, and violation-detectable (method: locate all three; threshold: all present; timeframe: review)
contraindications: stateless pure transforms
invariants already formally specified

neighbors.boundary: epistemic grades claim/evidence status; this audits the invariant inventory specifically
FMEA enumerates failure modes; this enumerates the correctness conditions failures would violate

entries.provenance: expansion-frontier; admission round 1 (2026-07-11, run wf_c747550c, evals/results-candidates-2026-07-11.md): caught its planted defect but produced NO unique P1/P2 vs the full active side (redundant basin) — remains candidate, admission gate not passed.

card.heuristic: Every system maintains invariants; almost none state them. The unstated invariant is one refactor away from silently false.
card.vector: Extract the implied invariants; for each demand statement, enforcement point, and violation alarm; flag the enforced-nowhere and detected-never.
card.bias: May formalize trivia; foreground the invariants whose violation is expensive and silent.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: the sabotage playbook overlap: the choice-set guaranteeing failure, matched against the actual plan; necessary conditions hunted for fragility
entries.required_evidence: the explicit saboteur playbook, each unintentional match to the real plan, the necessary-conditions list with fragility per condition
entries.causal_mechanism: paths to guaranteed failure are easier to enumerate than paths to success; the plan's overlap with its own sabotage playbook is its most legible risk list
canonical_questions: What would you do to make this fail on purpose — and are we doing any of it?
What must be true for this to work, and how fragile is each?
entries.falsifier_template: the flagged playbook match is absent from the plan (method: locate the practice in the plan; threshold: no match on inspection; timeframe: review)
contraindications: subject is a post-mortem (failure already known)
multi-participant pre-launch elicitation wanted (use the premortem PROTOCOL in the workflow methodology, not a lens)

neighbors.boundary: FMEA enumerates component failure modes of a SYSTEM; inversion enumerates choice-level failure paths of a PLAN

card.heuristic: Invert, always invert. It is often easier to find the path to guaranteed failure than the path to success — so find that path, and avoid it. What would you do if you *wanted* this to fail, and are you accidentally doing any of it?
card.vector: State the goal, then design its perfect sabotage: what set of choices would reliably produce the worst outcome? Compare that saboteur's playbook to the actual plan and flag every unintentional match. Ask "what must be true for this to work" and then hunt each necessary condition for fragility.
card.bias: Inversion can over-weight catastrophe; weigh the failure playbook against how many of its moves are actually live.

entries.notes: Absorbs the premortem stance for single-evaluator use; the premortem PROTOCOL (independent participant narratives before cross-talk) is a panel methodology option, not a lens.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: the right to ship what was built: license compatibility (copyleft in the closed build), model/data provenance rights, patent exposure on the core mechanism, trademark collisions
entries.required_evidence: the dependency license inventory with compatibility analysis, training-data/asset provenance, name clearance
entries.causal_mechanism: code and assets carry their licenses into the product silently; one copyleft dependency or unlicensed asset converts shipping into infringing
canonical_questions: Does every dependency's license permit this use and distribution?
Where did the training data / assets actually come from?
entries.falsifier_template: the flagged element's license/provenance permits the use (method: license text + provenance trace; threshold: compatible; timeframe: audit)
contraindications: internal-only artifacts with no distribution

neighbors.boundary: contracts allocate negotiated risk; this audits inherited IP rights
data-provenance traces integrity; this traces RIGHTS to the data/code

entries.provenance: expansion-frontier; admission round 1 (2026-07-11, run wf_c747550c, evals/results-candidates-2026-07-11.md): caught its planted defect but produced NO unique P1/P2 vs the full active side (redundant basin) — remains candidate, admission gate not passed.

card.heuristic: You do not own what you built; you own what your licenses let you ship. Every dependency is a contract you agreed to by importing it.
card.vector: Inventory licenses transitively; check compatibility with the distribution model; trace asset/model provenance; clear the name.
card.bias: May treat unenforced theoretical exposure as blocking; weigh against actual enforcement patterns.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: conflicting legal regimes on one system: data-residency vs processing location, conflicting disclosure obligations, the export that is legal on one side and illegal on the other
entries.required_evidence: the regime-per-data/operation map, identified conflicts, current safe-harbor/transfer mechanism validity
entries.causal_mechanism: cross-border systems accrete obligations from every jurisdiction they touch; some obligations conflict such that compliance with one is violation of another
canonical_questions: Which jurisdictions claim this data/operation, and do their demands conflict?
What transfer mechanism legitimizes this flow today?
entries.falsifier_template: the flow has a currently-valid legal basis in all claiming regimes (method: per-regime basis check; threshold: valid everywhere; timeframe: review)
contraindications: single-jurisdiction systems

neighbors.boundary: regulator reads one regime adversarially; this finds CONFLICTS between regimes
contracts allocate party risk; this maps sovereign claims

entries.provenance: expansion-frontier; PROBATION via admission round 1 (2026-07-11, run wf_c747550c, evals/results-candidates-2026-07-11.md): unique P2s: Schrems-II sovereign-access conflict persists even with SCCs; OFAC vs EU Blocking Statute two-master conflict (c2). Full activation per lifecycle rules (20 eligible live runs / quarterly review).

card.heuristic: Two sovereigns, one dataset, incompatible demands. Cross-border compliance is not a checklist; it is a conflict-of-laws problem wearing one.
card.vector: Map every regime touching each data class and operation; find the pairs whose demands conflict; check the transfer mechanism is still alive.
card.bias: May treat theoretical conflicts as live; weigh against enforcement reality and actual data flows.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: costs outside the decision boundary: creation-to-disposal externalities, the maintenance/disposal burden on parties not in the room, end-of-life obligations nobody scheduled
entries.required_evidence: the lifecycle stage inventory with per-stage cost bearer, the externalized burden list, end-of-life plan presence
entries.causal_mechanism: decisions optimize inside their boundary; lifecycle costs land on future operators, neighbors, and successors who never consented
canonical_questions: Who pays at each lifecycle stage, including disposal?
What happens to this when it is abandoned?
entries.falsifier_template: the flagged external cost is internalized or consented (method: trace bearer + consent; threshold: both; timeframe: assessment)
contraindications: self-contained short-lived artifacts

neighbors.boundary: justice disaggregates PRESENT impact; this traces impact across the artifact's LIFETIME
architect designs for long-term operability; this audits the full cost chain including abandonment

entries.provenance: expansion-frontier; admission round 1 (2026-07-11, run wf_c747550c, evals/results-candidates-2026-07-11.md): caught its planted defect but produced NO unique P1/P2 vs the full active side (redundant basin) — remains candidate, admission gate not passed.

card.heuristic: Every artifact is a future liability wearing its launch outfit. The full cost includes the years nobody wants it and the day someone must dispose of it.
card.vector: Walk creation→operation→abandonment→disposal; attribute each stage's cost to its actual bearer; flag the externalized and the unplanned end-of-life.
card.bias: May price speculative far-future burdens too heavily; discount honestly but do not zero them.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: cash-timing truth: runway under honest burn, receivable-vs-payable timing gaps, the commitment that is survivable on the income statement and fatal in the cash account
entries.required_evidence: cash-flow timeline with commitments, timing gaps, covenant/trigger dates
entries.causal_mechanism: solvent-on-paper entities die of timing; obligations due before receipts arrive end operations regardless of eventual profitability
canonical_questions: When exactly does cash cross zero under honest assumptions?
Which obligation lands before the income that covers it?
entries.falsifier_template: the cash timeline stays positive under the stress case (method: monthly cash model with stated stresses; threshold: no negative crossing; timeframe: model horizon)
contraindications: no material cash constraint in scope

neighbors.boundary: unit-economics attacks per-unit margin; this attacks cash TIMING regardless of margin
black-swan hunts unlisted tails; this computes the listed obligations' arithmetic

entries.provenance: expansion-frontier; admission round 1 (2026-07-11, run wf_c747550c, evals/results-candidates-2026-07-11.md): caught its planted defect but produced NO unique P1/P2 vs the full active side (redundant basin) — remains candidate, admission gate not passed.

card.heuristic: Profitable companies die of payroll dates. Cash has a calendar, and the calendar does not negotiate.
card.vector: Build the month-by-month cash line; overlay every commitment's due date; stress receipts by the honest slip; find the zero crossing.
card.bias: May over-conserve against fundable gaps; distinguish fatal timing from bridgeable timing.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: external-availability dependencies assumed permanent: cloud, SaaS, identity provider, CA, DNS as hard preconditions
entries.required_evidence: dependency inventory with offline-behavior per item, key/data custody map, degradation paths
entries.causal_mechanism: delegated dependencies fail on their schedule, not yours; on the worst day the vendor, CA, and cloud are unreachable exactly when needed
canonical_questions: What still works when the internet/IdP/vendor is gone?
Who holds the keys to your own data?
entries.falsifier_template: the flagged dependency has a working offline/degraded path (method: pull-the-plug test; threshold: core function survives; timeframe: drill)
positive_signals: trust anchors held by third parties
contraindications: operational-leverage requirements dominate (counter-mode: cloud-native-purist)
genuinely no self-host option

neighbors.boundary: MUTEX counter-mode: sovereignty-via-ownership vs leverage-via-delegation; pair only intentionally, count as one diversity unit

card.heuristic: The internet, the cloud, the SaaS vendor, and the CA can all be unavailable on the day you need them most. Sovereignty = local copy + offline workflow + revocable trust.
card.vector: Find dependencies on external availability that the design assumes is permanent; design for graceful degradation when cloud, identity provider, or DNS is gone; ensure you own the keys to your own data.
card.bias: Treats every SaaS as suspect; weigh against operational cost of self-hosting.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: the metric-goal proxy gap: how a rational actor games each steering metric while the true objective degrades; the unmeasured-because-hard
entries.required_evidence: per-metric true-objective statement, the named wedge, the concrete gaming path, the missing-metric inventory
entries.causal_mechanism: metrics-as-targets stop measuring (Goodhart); optimization pressure flows through the proxy gap and the real goal quietly degrades
canonical_questions: How do I hit this metric while making the goal worse?
What matters here but is unmeasured because it is hard?
entries.falsifier_template: the named gaming path does not move the metric or is detected (method: simulate/observe gaming attempt; threshold: metric robust or alarm fires; timeframe: test)
contraindications: no metric in the steering loop
metric already validated against the objective

neighbors.boundary: forecaster covers all displaced-consequence classes; measurement-critic owns the metric-proxy class exclusively
stats critic attacks whether the number is TRUE; measurement-critic attacks whether the true number MEANS the goal

card.heuristic: When a measure becomes a target, it ceases to be a good measure. Every metric is a proxy, every proxy has a gap, and the system will optimize the proxy straight through the gap while the real goal quietly degrades.
card.vector: For each metric that steers the design, name the true objective it stands in for and the wedge between them — then describe how a rational actor games the metric while worsening the goal (Goodhart's law, made concrete). Find the thing that matters but is not measured because it is hard, and the thing that is measured only because it is easy.
card.bias: May reject useful-if-imperfect metrics in pursuit of an unmeasurable ideal; weigh against the cost of flying blind.

card.heuristic: A confident-sounding claim with no error bars is a hazard. Knowledge claims must be paired with their failure modes.
card.vector: Distinguish what is measured vs. modeled vs. asserted; surface where uncertainty is hidden by polish; check whether the proposal would update on contrary evidence and what that evidence would look like.
card.bias: May paralyze with epistemic humility where action is required; weigh against operator's decision deadline.

entries.notes: Merged 2026-07-10 into epistemic-auditor v2: claim/evidence/status grading and uncertainty/update-threshold auditing are one diagnostic act on one object (the claim inventory), not two lenses.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: deletable existing capability: unused flexibility abstractions, undecided config knobs, features nobody would rebuild today
entries.required_evidence: usage data per feature/knob, abstraction instantiation counts, liability surface per retained element
entries.causal_mechanism: every retained element is a permanent liability; unused capability costs security, comprehension, and maintenance forever while returning nothing
canonical_questions: What here could be deleted instead of maintained?
Which abstraction exists for flexibility never exercised?
entries.falsifier_template: the deletion candidate has verified live users/value (method: usage telemetry or dependency check; threshold: nonzero material use; timeframe: recent window)
contraindications: already-minimal artifact
deletion decisions needing incident history (route to chesterton-gate)

neighbors.boundary: sentinel blocks scope being ADDED to a plan; zen-master deletes capability that already EXISTS
chesterton-gate adjudicates one specific proposed deletion against its origin evidence; zen-master generates deletion candidates

card.heuristic: The cheapest, most reliable, most secure feature is the one that doesn't exist. Every line of code, every config knob, every dependency is a permanent liability.
card.vector: Find capability that could be deleted instead of refactored; collapse abstractions that exist for theoretical flexibility never used; remove options that exist because no one decided.
card.bias: Will recommend deleting things with legitimate users; weigh against actual usage data.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: value-vs-adoption curve shape: compounding node-adds-edges vs flat tool vs degrading congestion; cold-start and tipping points
entries.required_evidence: same-side/cross-side value mechanism per user class, cold-start plan, congestion/moderation load projections
entries.causal_mechanism: confusing flat or degrading adoption curves with compounding ones mis-prices the whole strategy; the network story must name its edge mechanism
canonical_questions: Does each new user make it better or worse for the others?
What is the honest cold-start path?
entries.falsifier_template: the claimed network mechanism appears in usage data (method: cohort value vs network size; threshold: positive coupling; timeframe: measurement window)
contraindications: single-user tool with no interaction surface
no growth thesis at stake

neighbors.boundary: unit-economics attacks per-unit margin; strategist attacks the adoption-value curve shape

card.heuristic: Some value compounds with adoption and some doesn't, and confusing the two kills strategy. The question isn't "is it good?" but "does each new user make it better or worse for the others?"
card.vector: Identify whether value scales with the network (each node adds edges), stays flat (a tool), or degrades (congestion, noise, moderation load). Find the cold-start problem and the tipping point. Surface where a small design change turns a linear product into a compounding one — or reveals that the network story is wishful.
card.bias: May invent network effects where none exist; weigh against honest evidence of same-side/cross-side value.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: the do-nothing option, steelmanned: what the status quo quietly gets right, the honest cost of NOT acting, whether 'we must do something' is doing the persuasive work
entries.required_evidence: what currently works that a replacement must re-earn, the measured (not asserted) cost of inaction, the effort's alternative destination
entries.causal_mechanism: every proposal is scored against a phantom untenable status quo; the null option is the most under-considered alternative and usually gets no defense
canonical_questions: What is the status quo quietly getting right?
Is inaction a bleeding wound or a manageable annoyance dressed as crisis?
entries.falsifier_template: the measured cost of inaction exceeds the stated threshold (method: quantify the bleed; threshold: operator's action threshold; timeframe: assessment)
positive_signals: EVERY open-question run (the null option is mandatory in the option set)
contraindications: null already rigorously priced and rejected on evidence
fixed-artifact gate where doing-nothing is out of scope

neighbors.boundary: liberator adjudicates not-CONTINUING an existing effort; null-advocate generates the not-STARTING option
accountant compares against the best ACTIVE alternative; null-advocate defends the INACTIVE one

card.heuristic: The most under-considered alternative is always "do nothing / change nothing." Every proposal is scored against a phantom status quo that is painted as untenable, when often it is merely unglamorous and perfectly survivable. The default deserves its strongest defense, and usually gets none.
card.vector: Steelman the status quo: what is it quietly getting right that the replacement will have to re-earn? What breaks that currently works? Compute the honest cost of *not* acting — is it a real bleeding wound or a manageable annoyance dressed up as a crisis? Find where "we have to do something" is doing the persuasive work that evidence should. Name the version where the best move is to leave it alone and spend the effort elsewhere.
card.bias: Status-quo bias can ossify genuine stagnation; weigh the null option against the real, compounding cost of inaction — not a strawman of it.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: pre-incident signal design: alerts that fire before users notice, traces that survive async boundaries, logs that discriminate causes
entries.required_evidence: alert-coverage-per-failure-mode map, trace continuity check, log discriminability review
entries.causal_mechanism: unobserved systems are debugged by redeploy-and-pray at 3am by someone who didn't build them; the missing log line is the incident's real cost
canonical_questions: When this breaks, how does anyone know, and find out why?
Which failure mode has zero telemetry?
entries.falsifier_template: the named failure mode produces an actionable signal (method: fault injection + observe; threshold: alert + discriminating context; timeframe: test)
contraindications: telemetry already saturating the failure surface
no runtime to observe

neighbors.boundary: on-call-realist evaluates the human recovery workflow (runbook, rollback ergonomics); advocate evaluates the signal/telemetry substrate
forensicist requires investigation-grade integrity (chain of custody, retention) post-compromise; advocate requires operational debuggability pre/mid-incident

card.heuristic: You cannot operate what you cannot see, and every system is eventually operated by someone who didn't build it, at 3am, with a customer on the line. Debuggability is a first-class feature, not an afterthought.
card.vector: Ask "when this breaks, how will anyone know, and how will they find out why?" — the signal that fires *before* users notice, the trace that survives across the async boundary, the log line that says which of five things went wrong, the state you can inspect without a redeploy. Design for the incident, not just the happy path.
card.bias: May gold-plate telemetry beyond the failure surface that matters; weigh against instrumentation cost and noise.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: incident-time recovery ergonomics: actionable alerts, current runbooks, one-command rollback, half-asleep safety
entries.required_evidence: runbook currency check, rollback command inventory, alert actionability review, blast-radius containment
entries.causal_mechanism: recovery requiring the author, a fresh brain, or unrunbooked judgment converts a component failure into an extended outage
canonical_questions: Can a stranger fix this half-asleep without making it worse?
Is the rollback one command?
entries.falsifier_template: a non-author completes recovery from the runbook alone (method: game-day drill; threshold: MTTR within target, no escalation; timeframe: drill)
contraindications: nothing pages anyone
recovery fully automated and rehearsed

neighbors.boundary: advocate owns the signals; realist owns the human response those signals trigger
resilience-engineer designs automatic absorption; realist designs the manual path when automation ends
bus-factor attacks knowledge monopoly broadly; realist attacks the specific 3am recovery workflow

card.heuristic: The real design review is the pager at 3am. If recovery requires the author, a fresh brain, or a decision under pressure that a runbook can't carry, it isn't done — it's a future incident with a countdown.
card.vector: Walk the worst realistic failure as the person who gets paged with no context: is the alert actionable, is the runbook current, is the rollback one command, is the blast radius contained, can they fix it half-asleep without making it worse? Design the recovery to be boring. Prefer automatic healing to heroic response.
card.bias: May demand operational maturity disproportionate to the stakes; weigh against real severity and frequency.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: the invisible alternative: what the same time/attention/capital would buy elsewhere; the crowded-out higher-leverage work
entries.required_evidence: named best-alternative uses with rough value, attention/maintenance surface this permanently adds
entries.causal_mechanism: proposals are argued against 'nothing' while the real comparator is the best alternative; the displaced option never appears on the page
canonical_questions: What is the best alternative use of these exact resources?
Is this the best thing, or merely a good thing?
entries.falsifier_template: the proposal beats the named best alternative (method: like-for-like value comparison; threshold: proposal wins; timeframe: analysis)
contraindications: resources genuinely uncontended
alternative-space already adjudicated by operator

neighbors.boundary: null-advocate generates and steelmans the do-nothing option; accountant compares against the best ACTIVE alternative
unit-economics attacks the path's own margin; opportunity-cost attacks its ranking among paths

card.heuristic: The cost of doing this is everything you can no longer do because you did it. The proposal is always compared against "nothing," but the real comparison is against the best alternative use of the same time, attention, and capital.
card.vector: Name what this crowds out — the higher-leverage work that will not happen, the team-attention this monopolizes, the maintenance surface it permanently adds. Ask "is this the best thing we could build with these resources, or just a good thing?" Surface the invisible alternative the proposal never mentions because it was never on the page.
card.bias: Everything has an opportunity cost, so this can veto anything; weigh against the value of actually shipping something.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: the rejected branch, given its best advocate: the strongest case for the road not taken, compared like-for-like against the chosen path
entries.required_evidence: the alternative's smartest-advocate case, the rejection's load-bearing assumption checked, symmetric costs the rejection ignored
entries.causal_mechanism: the winner was argued and the loser was only mentioned; the strongest case for the rejected branch frequently beats the case that dismissed it
canonical_questions: What is the best possible case for the alternative this rejects?
Did the rejection rest on an assumption that does not hold?
entries.falsifier_template: the like-for-like comparison still favors the chosen path (method: matched-criteria comparison; threshold: chosen path wins on the decisive axes; timeframe: analysis)
positive_signals: proposal dismisses alternatives in a sentence
contraindications: alternatives already given full trials
no rejected branch exists to advocate

neighbors.boundary: explorer searches the unexamined neighborhood; steelman advocates the explicitly-rejected branch
null-advocate defends doing nothing; steelman defends doing the OTHER thing

card.heuristic: The proposal chose a fork, and the rejected branch was almost never given a fair trial — it was dismissed in a sentence. The strongest case for the road not taken is frequently stronger than the case that beat it, because the winner was argued and the loser was only mentioned.
card.vector: Take the alternative the proposal rejects (or never surfaced) and build the best possible case *for* it — not a caricature, the version its smartest advocate would give. Then compare like-for-like against the chosen path. Surface where the rejection rested on an assumption that does not hold, a cost that is actually symmetric, or a strength of the alternative that went unmentioned. Force a real contest, not a coronation.
card.bias: Can make a genuinely-worse alternative sound plausible through advocacy alone; label the steelman as advocacy and let the comparison, not the rhetoric, decide.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: current hot-path inefficiency: N+1 queries, sync network calls, inner-loop allocations, missing indexes, tail-latency sources
entries.required_evidence: profiles, query plans, P99 latency measurements, allocation traces
entries.causal_mechanism: mean-optimized systems hide tail latency; the P99 experience is the reliability users actually feel
canonical_questions: Where is the N+1 or sync-across-network in the hot path?
What drives P99 vs the mean?
entries.falsifier_template: the flagged path measures within budget (method: profile/benchmark; threshold: P99 within SLO; timeframe: measurement)
contraindications: cold paths with no workload evidence
subject with no runtime surface

neighbors.boundary: cliff-analyst finds FUTURE discontinuities at scale; alchemist optimizes MEASURED current paths

card.heuristic: Latency is a feature; tail latency is reliability. P99 matters more than mean.
card.vector: Profile hot paths; find N+1 queries, sync calls across the network, allocations in inner loops, missing indexes, serialization formats that don't fit the access pattern.
card.bias: May optimize cold paths; weigh against actual workload and SLOs.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: the category of the question itself: whether the subject answers the question that matters, tested via cross-domain reframings
entries.required_evidence: the unspoken question the subject answers, at least one cross-field analogy that redefines 'good' here
entries.causal_mechanism: solving the wrong problem well wastes everything downstream; category errors are invisible from inside the frame that made them
canonical_questions: What question is this actually answering?
What does an adjacent field call this problem, and how do they solve it?
entries.falsifier_template: the reframing does not change the decision (method: re-derive the choice under the alternate frame; threshold: same choice survives; timeframe: analysis)
contraindications: frame already contested and settled by operator ruling
mechanical execution subject with fixed frame

neighbors.boundary: premise-auditor excavates unstated premises WITHIN the given frame; inquisitor challenges the frame's category itself
historian reports how the same structural shape actually played out; inquisitor uses analogy to reframe, not to predict

card.heuristic: Most failures are category errors — solving the wrong problem well. Question the framing before the implementation.
card.vector: Identify the unspoken question the subject is answering; ask whether that's the question that matters; surface analogies from other fields that reframe what "good" looks like here.
card.bias: Risk of clever reframings that dissolve real problems into philosophy; weigh against operator's actual constraint set.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: the record: verified lens reports and their conflicts — no new evidence admitted at synthesis time
entries.required_evidence: the conflict ledger inputs: verified findings, evidence tiers, falsifier check results
entries.causal_mechanism: rhetoric volume distorts unweighted synthesis; ruling on evidence quality with dissent preserved keeps the verdict computable and auditable
canonical_questions: Which side of each conflict does the verified evidence weight support?
What ruling does the P1-P4 gate compute?
entries.falsifier_template: a ruling cites evidence not in the record, or the computed verdict contradicts the gate logic (method: audit rulings against record + gate; threshold: any violation; timeframe: audit)
positive_signals: DEFAULT final judge for every panel
contraindications: defensible priors and likelihoods exist and probabilistic ruling is wanted (alternate: bayesian-adjudicator)

neighbors.boundary: bayesian is the ALTERNATE final judge only when defensible priors/likelihoods exist; pragmatic-judge is the evidence-weighted default
synthesizer generates pre-judgment synthesis candidates; it never rules — judge consumes its candidates

card.heuristic: Decide the case on the record. No new evidence at synthesis time. Weight by evidence quality, not rhetoric volume.
card.vector: Build the Conflict Ledger. For each disagreement: identify the experts in tension, summarize the conflict, weigh evidence, rule UPHELD / OVERRULED / SPLIT with justification keyed to verified evidence tags.
card.bias: May default to compromise rather than choose; force a ruling unless evidence genuinely splits.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: gaps between written policy/disclosure and actual practice under an adversarial literal reading
entries.required_evidence: policies, disclosures, consent flows, audit-trail presence, retention configs vs stated regime
entries.causal_mechanism: an enforcement-motivated reader treats every defensible-but-misleading statement and policy-practice gap as a violation exhibit
canonical_questions: Which stated control does practice not implement?
Which consent flow fails a literal reading of the regime?
entries.falsifier_template: policy statement matches observed practice (method: sample practice against policy text; threshold: no material divergence; timeframe: audit sample)
contraindications: purely internal artifact under no regime
no user data and no disclosure surface

neighbors.boundary: litigator mines internal records as future exhibits; regulator reads external disclosures and controls
privacy critic evaluates data practices as harm; regulator evaluates them as enforceable violations

card.heuristic: A regulator with a quota, a press release, and 18 months. Reads every disclosure adversarially.
card.vector: Find statements that are technically defensible but materially misleading, control gaps between policy and practice, missing logs that should exist under the relevant regime, consent flows that don't survive a literal reading.
card.bias: Treats "spirit of compliance" defenses as worthless; may flag findings a friendly regulator would never pursue.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: how the verdict moves under defensible preference weightings: which conclusions are preference-robust vs preference-hostage, the weighting the recommendation silently assumes
entries.required_evidence: the verdict recomputed under alternative defensible weightings, the sensitivity map, the silently-assumed weighting exposed
entries.causal_mechanism: recommendations embed value weightings presented as analysis; conclusions that flip under defensible re-weighting are choices, not findings
canonical_questions: Under which defensible preference weightings does this conclusion flip?
What weighting is the recommendation silently assuming?
entries.falsifier_template: the conclusion is stable across the defensible weighting range (method: sensitivity sweep; threshold: no flip in range; timeframe: analysis)
contraindications: single-criterion decisions with no weighting freedom

neighbors.boundary: ruler applies the operator's RECORDED values; this maps which conclusions DEPEND on values at all
bayesian sweeps priors over facts; this sweeps weights over values

entries.provenance: expansion-frontier; admission round 1 (2026-07-11, run wf_c747550c, evals/results-candidates-2026-07-11.md): caught its planted defect but produced NO unique P1/P2 vs the full active side (redundant basin) — remains candidate, admission gate not passed.

card.heuristic: Every multi-criteria verdict smuggles a weighting. Conclusions that survive any defensible weighting are findings; the rest are preferences wearing lab coats.
card.vector: Recompute the recommendation under alternative defensible weightings; map which conclusions are robust and which are hostage to a specific weighting nobody declared.
card.bias: Sensitivity sweeps can manufacture indecision; when the operator's weighting IS recorded, apply it rather than relitigating it.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: load-bearing unstated premises WITHIN the given frame: ranked by how much rests on them and how shaky they are; the single assumption whose falsity collapses the case
entries.required_evidence: the excavated premise list with verified/inherited status per premise, the load ranking, what the design becomes if the top premise is false
entries.causal_mechanism: debate rages over conclusions while the settling premise sits invisible underneath; changing the premise moves the whole edifice
canonical_questions: Which unstated assumption carries the most load?
Is it verified, or inherited — and has anyone checked?
entries.falsifier_template: the flagged premise verifies (method: direct check of the premise; threshold: holds under test; timeframe: verification)
contraindications: the question's FRAME itself is the doubt (route to polymath-inquisitor)
premises already enumerated and verified in the dossier

neighbors.boundary: inquisitor challenges the frame's category; premise-auditor audits premises WITHIN the accepted frame
epistemic-auditor grades STATED claims; premise-auditor excavates UNSTATED ones
semantic-critic targets the words carrying claims; premise-auditor targets the propositions beneath them

card.heuristic: A proposal is a conclusion resting on premises, and the premises are usually load-bearing, unstated, and unexamined. The debate rages over the conclusion while the premise that would settle it sits invisible underneath. Change the premise and the whole edifice moves.
card.vector: Excavate the assumptions the proposal takes as given — about the user, the scale, the threat, the future, what "success" means. For each, ask: is this actually true, is it verified or inherited, and what does the design become if it is false? Rank premises by how much rests on them and how shaky they are. Surface the single assumption whose falsity would collapse the entire case — and whether anyone has checked it.
card.bias: Can dissolve any proposal by questioning premises indefinitely; foreground the few load-bearing, actually-doubtful assumptions over the trivially-questionable many.

card.heuristic: It is twelve months from now and this failed completely. The question is not *whether* — it's *how*, and everyone already knows, they just haven't said it. Prospective hindsight surfaces what optimism suppresses.
card.vector: Assume total failure as a fact, then enumerate the distinct causal stories that produce it, ranked by plausibility. Force each into a concrete narrative ("the day it broke, X had already been true for weeks"). Name the failure everyone will call "obvious in retrospect."
card.bias: Manufactures failure modes that won't occur; weigh each against base rate and the actual control surface.

entries.notes: Retired as a standalone evaluator 2026-07-10: single-agent prospective hindsight duplicates inversion-thinker's mechanism. The distinct value of a premortem — independent participant narratives elicited BEFORE cross-talk — is a PANEL METHODOLOGY (the gauntlet's independent-lens barrier already implements it) and is documented in reference/execution-model.md, not a lens card.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: data-practice harm: collected-because-possible data, purpose-outliving retention, profile joinability, log exposure, function creep into tracking
entries.required_evidence: per-datum necessity vs possibility classification, retention vs purpose, joinability analysis, processor exposure map
entries.causal_mechanism: collected data eventually leaks, gets subpoenaed, or is repurposed; surveillance infrastructure accretes one reasonable step at a time
canonical_questions: Is each datum collected because needed or because possible?
What does a future owner or adversary do with this store?
entries.falsifier_template: the flagged datum has demonstrated necessity + bounded retention (method: purpose trace + retention config; threshold: both verified; timeframe: audit)
contraindications: no personal data in scope
data practice already minimized and audited

neighbors.boundary: dual-use targets capability repurposing; privacy critic targets data accumulation/repurposing
regulator evaluates practices as enforceable violations; privacy critic evaluates them as harm regardless of regime
ethicist defers data-practice harm to this lens

card.heuristic: Data collected is data that will eventually leak, be subpoenaed, be repurposed, or be used to build a profile no one consented to. The privacy-invasive default is the one that ships because it is easier, and "we might need it later" is how surveillance infrastructure gets built one reasonable step at a time.
card.vector: Trace every piece of personal or behavioral data the system touches: is it collected because it is needed or because it is possible, retained longer than its purpose, joinable into a profile, exposed in a log, shared with a processor whose posture is unknown? Find the function creep — the benign-purpose datum that becomes a tracking vector. Ask what an adversary (or a future owner) does with this store.
card.bias: May treat all data collection as suspect; weigh against genuine necessity and the real sensitivity of the specific data.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: reconstruction of why the current state exists: the decision/compat/version history behind present structure — WITHOUT any proposed deletion in scope
entries.required_evidence: commit/decision archaeology, version-constraint provenance, the incident or consumer each oddity served
entries.causal_mechanism: 'we should just' proposals fail against constraints whose reasons were forgotten; reconstructing provenance converts mystery into evaluable constraint
canonical_questions: What produced this structure, step by step?
Which current consumers/incidents does each oddity serve?
entries.falsifier_template: the reconstructed history is contradicted by the record (method: check archaeology against commits/ADRs/incidents; threshold: material contradiction; timeframe: records check)
contraindications: HARD BOUNDARY: a specific deletion is being adjudicated (that is chesterton-gate's exclusive job)
greenfield with no history

neighbors.boundary: chesterton-gate approves/blocks ONE specific proposed deletion on origin-obsolescence evidence; archeologist reconstructs history with no deletion in scope — enforced boundary, not a preference

card.heuristic: Every "we should just" hides a decade of decisions that produced the current state. Understand why before you change.
card.vector: Reconstruct the historical reasons for the present structure; identify Chesterton's fences (constraints, exceptions, weird workarounds) and demand justification for removing them.
card.bias: Conservatism toward change; weigh against legitimate need to break with the past.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: queueing behavior under sustained load: arrival-vs-service rate, backpressure paths, retry storms, the metastable overload that persists after the trigger clears
entries.required_evidence: arrival/service rate estimates, queue growth arithmetic, retry amplification factors, load-shedding presence
entries.causal_mechanism: when arrival exceeds service, queues grow without bound; retries amplify overload into a metastable state that outlives its cause
canonical_questions: Where does arrival rate exceed service rate, even briefly?
What does the retry storm do to the recovering service?
entries.falsifier_template: the queue drains after the stated overload (method: load model or test at k×nominal; threshold: returns to steady state; timeframe: test)
contraindications: strictly synchronous request-bounded systems

neighbors.boundary: cliff finds where cost curves snap; this audits queue/feedback dynamics under overload
resilience designs degraded modes; this attacks the queue math those modes must survive

entries.provenance: expansion-frontier; admission round 1 (2026-07-11, run wf_c747550c, evals/results-candidates-2026-07-11.md): caught its planted defect but produced NO unique P1/P2 vs the full active side (redundant basin) — remains candidate, admission gate not passed.

card.heuristic: A queue is a promise that service rate exceeds arrival rate. Break the promise for a minute and the queue remembers for an hour.
card.vector: Do the rate arithmetic; trace what backpressure actually propagates; multiply the retry amplification; hunt the metastable state.
card.bias: May model spikes the workload cannot produce; anchor rates in measured traffic.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: restore-path truth: backup completeness/consistency, restore drill recency, RPO/RTO arithmetic, the dependency needed for restore that dies with the primary
entries.required_evidence: last successful RESTORE (not backup) evidence, backup content verification, restore-path dependency closure
entries.causal_mechanism: backups succeed and restores fail; the restore path silently depends on infrastructure that the disaster also takes out
canonical_questions: When did a restore last actually succeed, end to end?
What does the restore path need that the disaster removes?
entries.falsifier_template: a full restore drill succeeds within RTO (method: drill; threshold: RTO/RPO met from real backup; timeframe: drill)
positive_signals: root-of-trust material (CA keys, at-rest encryption keys)
contraindications: stateless rebuildable-from-source systems

neighbors.boundary: common-cause intersects redundant paths generally; this audits the backup/restore path specifically
on-call drills incident response; this drills data restoration integrity

entries.provenance: expansion-frontier; PROBATION via admission round 1 (2026-07-11, run wf_c747550c, evals/results-candidates-2026-07-11.md): unique P1: independent per-shard dumps cannot yield a consistent restore point -> invariant unrestorable (c1). Full activation per lifecycle rules (20 eligible live runs / quarterly review).

card.heuristic: 'We have backups' is unverified until a restore succeeds. The backup you have not restored from is a hope with a retention policy.
card.vector: Demand the last restore drill date and result; verify backup content against source; trace what the restore path itself depends on.
card.bias: Full-restore drills are expensive; scale frequency to data irreplaceability, not uniformly.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: categorical bounds BEFORE optimization: whether any surviving recommendation crosses a non-negotiable (safety, consent, legality, irreversibility, recorded Sovereign values)
entries.required_evidence: the enumerated red lines with their categorical justification, each surviving recommendation checked against each line
entries.causal_mechanism: optimization happily trades a non-negotiable for efficiency; a breach must gate the path out entirely, not enter the weighing
canonical_questions: Which hard constraints are in play, and is each truly categorical?
Does any surviving path cross one?
entries.falsifier_template: a declared red line is shown to be a tradeable preference (method: demand the categorical justification; threshold: justification fails; timeframe: review)
contraindications: no plausible categorical constraint in play
all paths already inside known bounds

neighbors.boundary: lawyer gates panel procedure; red-lines gates subject recommendations against categorical bounds
ethicist EVALUATES values tensions as findings; red-lines GATES on the operator's declared categorical lines

card.heuristic: Some constraints are not to be optimized against — they are the boundary of the playing field. A verdict that trades a non-negotiable (safety, consent, legality, irreversibility, the Sovereign's stated values) for efficiency is not a clever trade-off; it is out of bounds, no matter how favorable the math.
card.vector: Before weighing the optimization conflicts, identify the hard constraints in play and check whether any surviving recommendation crosses one. A red-line breach is an automatic NO-GO on that path regardless of its other merits — record it as a gate, not a factor. Distinguish true red lines (categorical) from strong preferences (tradeable); over-declaring red lines makes the category meaningless.
card.bias: May elevate a strong preference to a false absolute and foreclose a legitimate trade; require each red line to be justified as categorical, not merely important.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: the cutover event itself: sequencing, feature-flag states, cache/CDN staleness, DNS propagation, the half-deployed state's behavior
entries.required_evidence: the cutover runbook step sequence, per-step failure behavior, the half-done state's user-visible behavior
entries.causal_mechanism: releases fail in the seams between steps; the half-deployed state is a configuration nobody designed or tested
canonical_questions: What do users see between step 3 and step 4?
Which step, failing, leaves an unrecoverable half-state?
entries.falsifier_template: each intermediate state is designed and survivable (method: step-through rehearsal with mid-sequence abort; threshold: every abort recoverable; timeframe: rehearsal)
contraindications: atomic deploys with no intermediate states

neighbors.boundary: migration audits data/schema across the window; this audits operational event sequencing
on-call audits incident recovery generally; this audits the planned cutover's own failure modes

entries.provenance: expansion-frontier; PROBATION via admission round 1 (2026-07-11, run wf_c747550c, evals/results-candidates-2026-07-11.md): unique P1: step3->4 dual-write split-brain window + no per-step abort criteria (c1). Full activation per lifecycle rules (20 eligible live runs / quarterly review).

card.heuristic: The deploy is a distributed transaction without a coordinator. Every step boundary is a state users can be served from.
card.vector: Walk the runbook step by step asking 'and if it stops HERE?'; check flag/cache/DNS skew windows; find the abort that cannot roll back.
card.bias: May demand ceremony for trivially-atomic releases; scale rigor to the blast radius of the half-state.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: the requirement-to-implementation-to-verification chain: orphan requirements nothing implements, orphan code no requirement justifies, verifications testing nothing required
entries.required_evidence: the three-way trace matrix, orphans in each direction
entries.causal_mechanism: requirements drift from implementation silently; the unimplemented requirement and the unjustified implementation both hide in the gap between documents
canonical_questions: Which requirement has no implementing artifact?
Which artifact serves no stated requirement?
entries.falsifier_template: the flagged chain link exists (method: trace the specific link; threshold: located; timeframe: audit)
contraindications: exploratory work with deliberately-emergent requirements

neighbors.boundary: sentinel blocks scope ADDITIONS; this audits the existing requirement-artifact correspondence
invariant-auditor targets correctness conditions; this targets requirement coverage

entries.provenance: expansion-frontier; admission round 1 (2026-07-11, run wf_c747550c, evals/results-candidates-2026-07-11.md): caught its planted defect but produced NO unique P1/P2 vs the full active side (redundant basin) — remains candidate, admission gate not passed.

card.heuristic: The spec and the system diverge from the day both exist. Traceability is the only instrument that sees the divergence before the acceptance dispute does.
card.vector: Build the requirement→artifact→verification matrix; hunt orphans in all three directions; date the last time anyone reconciled them.
card.bias: Full traceability is bureaucracy for exploratory work; apply where requirements are contractual or safety-relevant.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: degraded-mode design: what 'partially working' means per subsystem, fallbacks, circuit breakers, rehearsed recovery
entries.required_evidence: failure-mode-to-degraded-behavior map, breaker/bulkhead configs, recovery rehearsal records
entries.causal_mechanism: reliability is absorption, not absence, of failure; systems without designed degraded modes fail total when they fail at all
canonical_questions: What does this subsystem do when its dependency is down?
Has the recovery ever been rehearsed?
entries.falsifier_template: the named degraded mode works as designed (method: fault-injection rehearsal; threshold: degraded behavior matches spec; timeframe: drill)
contraindications: scale/blast-radius too small to justify the machinery
pure document subject

neighbors.boundary: chaos-monkey adversarially hunts coupling that defeats redundancy; resilience-engineer constructively designs the degraded modes
on-call-realist targets the human recovery experience; resilience-engineer targets the system's automatic absorption

card.heuristic: Reliability is not the absence of failure; it is the ability to absorb it. Build degraded modes, not just nominal ones.
card.vector: Define what "partially working" means for each subsystem; design fallbacks, circuit breakers, bulkheads; ensure recovery is rehearsed, not theoretical.
card.bias: May add complexity for failure modes that won't occur at this scale; weigh against system size and blast radius.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: the decision's undo class: reversible / reversible-at-cost / irreversible; hidden ratchets converting reversible into permanent
entries.required_evidence: undo-path inventory per consequence, the specific ratchet (data written, trust extended, dependents built), effort-vs-class match
entries.causal_mechanism: most bad calls mis-class the door: one-way doors rushed or two-way doors deliberated; hidden ratchets flip the class silently after commitment
canonical_questions: Can this be undone, at what cost, until when?
What ratchet quietly makes this permanent?
entries.falsifier_template: the named ratchet does not bind (method: demonstrate undo after the ratchet event; threshold: restoration within stated cost; timeframe: test)
contraindications: trivially reversible subject
reversibility already the explicit design axis

neighbors.boundary: black-swan targets tail loss magnitude; reversibility targets undo-class of the decision itself
sunk-cost audits the pull of past spend on continue/stop; reversibility audits the future undo path

card.heuristic: The single most decision-relevant property is whether you can undo it. One-way doors demand deliberation; two-way doors demand speed. Most bad calls are two-way doors treated as one-way (paralysis) or one-way doors treated as two-way (recklessness).
card.vector: Classify the decision: reversible, reversible-at-a-cost, or irreversible — and check whether the effort spent matches the class. Find the hidden ratchet (data written, trust extended, a dependency others now build on) that quietly converts a reversible choice into a permanent one. Surface the cheap experiment that would preserve optionality.
card.bias: May over-value optionality and never commit; weigh against the cost of indecision and the value of a burned bridge.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: performance across futures rather than in the expected one: the option that wins the forecast but dies off-forecast, regret asymmetry, robust-vs-optimal framing
entries.required_evidence: the option-by-scenario performance matrix, regret per cell, the forecast-dependence of the recommendation
entries.causal_mechanism: optimizing for the point forecast concentrates fragility; under deep uncertainty the question is which option survives the futures you did not pick
canonical_questions: How does each option perform in the futures where the forecast is wrong?
Which choice minimizes maximum regret?
entries.falsifier_template: the recommended option is non-dominated across the scenario set (method: scenario matrix; threshold: no alternative dominates across cells; timeframe: analysis)
contraindications: short-horizon reversible choices where the forecast barely matters

neighbors.boundary: black-swan hunts unlisted tails; this stress-tests options across LISTED rival futures
reversibility classes the undo path; this compares option performance across futures

entries.provenance: expansion-frontier; admission round 1 (2026-07-11, run wf_c747550c, evals/results-candidates-2026-07-11.md): caught its planted defect but produced NO unique P1/P2 vs the full active side (redundant basin) — remains candidate, admission gate not passed.

card.heuristic: The optimal plan is a bet on one future; the robust plan is a citizen of many. Under deep uncertainty, optimality is fragility with good marketing.
card.vector: Build the option×scenario matrix; compute regret; flag recommendations that only win in the house forecast; surface the robust runner-up.
card.bias: Robustness has a price in expected value; state the premium explicitly rather than defaulting to maximum caution.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: paths from system behavior to physical/bodily/environmental harm: energy sources, actuation, medical/safety-relevant outputs, foreseeable-misuse harm
entries.required_evidence: the hazard inventory with per-hazard severity, exposure, and mitigation layer count (STPA/hazard-analysis style)
entries.causal_mechanism: software reasoning stops at data loss; when outputs actuate or advise in the physical world, defect classes become injury classes
canonical_questions: What physical harm can any output or actuation cause?
How many independent layers sit between defect and injury?
entries.falsifier_template: the named hazard path has independent mitigation layers (method: layer-of-protection analysis; threshold: >=2 independent layers for severe hazards; timeframe: analysis)
contraindications: pure information systems with no physical/health coupling

neighbors.boundary: dual-use walks deliberate-abuse paths; this audits accidental/foreseeable harm paths
FMEA ranks component failures generally; this owns the failure-to-physical-harm chain

entries.provenance: expansion-frontier; admission round 1 (2026-07-11, run wf_c747550c, evals/results-candidates-2026-07-11.md): caught its planted defect but produced NO unique P1/P2 vs the full active side (redundant basin) — remains candidate, admission gate not passed.

card.heuristic: The severity scale changes kind, not degree, when a defect can reach a body. Data loss has backups; injuries do not.
card.vector: Trace every actuation/advice path to its worst physical outcome; count independent protection layers; assume foreseeable misuse.
card.bias: May import industrial-safety ceremony into harmless domains; confirm a physical/health coupling exists first.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: discontinuous scale breakpoints: O(n²) behind O(n) mental models, fan-out, coordination overhead, metadata outgrowing data
entries.required_evidence: growth quantities and their algorithmic/coordination cost, measured constants, the specific number where each assumption snaps
entries.causal_mechanism: cost curves that look linear at current n turn superlinear past a threshold, so the system works perfectly right up to a cliff
canonical_questions: Which quantity is quadratic behind a linear assumption?
At what specific n does this design's assumption snap?
entries.falsifier_template: the named quantity stays within budget at the projected scale (method: load model or measurement at 10x/100x; threshold: within capacity envelope; timeframe: projection window)
contraindications: scale honestly bounded and known
document/policy subject with no scaling surface

neighbors.boundary: alchemist optimizes current hot paths (constructive); cliff-analyst finds future discontinuities
unit-economics attacks per-unit cost viability; cliff-analyst attacks technical feasibility at scale

card.heuristic: Systems don't degrade linearly — they work fine, then fall off a cliff. The design that is elegant at 1x is a different organism at 100x, and the transition is discontinuous.
card.vector: Find the quantity that is O(n²) hiding behind an O(n) mental model — the fan-out, the all-pairs check, the coordination overhead, the lock contention, the metadata that grows faster than the data. Identify what breaks at 10x (load), 100x (architecture), 1000x (fundamental assumption). Name the specific number where each assumption snaps.
card.bias: May demand hyperscale readiness the system will never need; weigh against the honest growth curve.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: scope being ADDED to a plan: undecided requirements, gold-plating, the second system smuggled inside the first, the minimum coherent version
entries.required_evidence: requirement provenance (decided vs snuck-in), per-addition cost in time/risk/coupling, the minimum-coherent-version definition
entries.causal_mechanism: scope expands to fill ambition; each 'while we're at it' adds schedule risk and coupling until nothing ships
canonical_questions: Which requirement snuck in without a decision?
What is the minimum coherent version?
entries.falsifier_template: the flagged addition has a recorded decision and paid-for cost (method: locate the decision + estimate; threshold: both exist; timeframe: review)
contraindications: scope already frozen and minimal
cutting would ship something too thin to matter (declare, don't amputate)

neighbors.boundary: zen-master deletes EXISTING capability; sentinel blocks scope being ADDED to a plan
accountant ranks the whole effort against alternatives; sentinel polices additions within the chosen effort

card.heuristic: Scope is a gas — it expands to fill the ambition available. Every "while we are at it" is a schedule risk and a coupling, and the project that tries to do everything ships nothing on time.
card.vector: Separate the load-bearing core from the accreted "wouldn't it also be nice." Find the requirement that snuck in without a decision, the gold-plating on a path that does not need it, the second system smuggled inside the first. Ask what the minimum coherent version is and what each addition beyond it actually costs in time, risk, and coupling.
card.bias: May amputate genuinely necessary scope as creep; weigh against the cost of shipping something too thin to matter.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: internet-facing opportunistic attack surface: defaults, unpatched CVEs, exposed panels, leaked secrets
entries.required_evidence: exposure scan results, version/CVE mapping, credential defaults, public repo/bucket leak checks
entries.causal_mechanism: mass automated scanning monetizes any exposed default or known CVE within hours; obscurity provides no delay
canonical_questions: What is internet-reachable with default or weak auth?
Which exposed component has a stock exploit?
entries.falsifier_template: the named exposure is unreachable or patched (method: external scan/version check; threshold: no match on default-cred/CVE probe; timeframe: current scan)
contraindications: fully LAN-isolated subject with no exposure path
pure strategy/document subject

neighbors.boundary: opportunistic-mass vs targeted-persistent — controls that stop one do not stop the other
STRIDE walks the design's trust boundaries systematically; script-kiddie tests only the exposed-opportunistic slice

card.heuristic: Mass, low-skill, high-volume. Will hit anything internet-exposed with stock tooling within hours.
card.vector: Default credentials, unpatched CVEs in the first Shodan page of results, exposed admin panels, leaked secrets in public repos/buckets, weak auth on management interfaces.
card.bias: May under-rate sophisticated risks because the high-volume baseline noise drowns them out.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: mechanism-backed second/third-order consequence chains: gamed metrics, bred workarounds, displaced load, actor re-optimization
entries.required_evidence: incentive map of affected actors, named mechanism per hop, historical response to similar interventions
entries.causal_mechanism: an intervention perturbs surrounding incentives; actors re-optimize and the displaced behavior lands on an adjacent system the proposal never modeled
canonical_questions: Who re-optimizes around this rule, and how?
Which adjacent system absorbs the displaced load?
entries.falsifier_template: the predicted adaptation does not occur (method: named observable behavior/metric; threshold: absent after adoption; timeframe: stated horizon)
contraindications: single-actor mechanical change with no incentive surface
hop chains with no nameable mechanism

neighbors.boundary: game-theorist solves the equilibrium of modeled actors; forecaster traces dynamic adaptation chains beyond the model
measurement-critic targets the metric-goal proxy gap specifically; forecaster covers all displaced-consequence classes
logician analyzes feedback structure of the existing system; forecaster projects responses to the new intervention

card.heuristic: The first-order effect is the pitch; the second- and third-order effects are where systems die. Every intervention perturbs the incentives around it, and the perturbation is usually invisible in the proposal.
card.vector: Trace the Cobra Effect — where the fix creates the behavior it punishes. Ask "and then what?" three times past the stated outcome. Find the metric that will be gamed, the workaround the constraint will breed, the adjacent system that absorbs the displaced load, the actor who re-optimizes around the new rule.
card.bias: Can spin unfalsifiable chains of consequence; weigh each hop against a real mechanism, not vibes.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: load-bearing vocabulary: euphemism, category-blurring, terms the proposal avoids; whether plain restatement preserves the commitment
entries.required_evidence: catalog of load-bearing terms, plain-language restatement test results, avoided-term inventory
entries.causal_mechanism: words frame thought; a commitment that dissolves under plain restatement was carried by the vocabulary, not the argument
canonical_questions: Does this survive restatement in plain words?
What is this document careful never to say?
entries.falsifier_template: plain restatement preserves the claim's force (method: restate and compare commitments; threshold: no material shift; timeframe: analysis)
contraindications: mechanical/technical subject with settled vocabulary
no document surface

neighbors.boundary: premise-auditor targets unstated propositions; semantic-critic targets the words carrying stated ones

card.heuristic: Words frame thought. The terms a proposal uses — and the ones it avoids — reveal what the author cannot or will not see.
card.vector: Catalog the load-bearing vocabulary; identify euphemism, jargon, and category-blurring; test whether plain restatement preserves the original commitment.
card.bias: Treats linguistic precision as a goal in itself; weigh against operational clarity.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: the org-chart/system-architecture mirror: team boundaries vs component boundaries, the interface that is really a political treaty, ownership seams nobody patrols
entries.required_evidence: the team-to-component ownership map, cross-boundary change friction evidence, orphaned-seam inventory
entries.causal_mechanism: systems copy the communication structure that builds them (Conway); misaligned seams create components nobody owns and interfaces that encode politics
canonical_questions: Which component boundary is really a team boundary?
Which seam has no owner on either side?
entries.falsifier_template: the flagged seam has an owner and a working change path (method: trace a cross-seam change; threshold: completes without escalation; timeframe: trace)
contraindications: single-team systems where topology is trivial

neighbors.boundary: decision-rights audits authority; this audits the structural mirror between org and architecture
logician maps feedback loops; this maps the org-to-artifact homomorphism

entries.provenance: expansion-frontier; admission round 1 (2026-07-11, run wf_c747550c, evals/results-candidates-2026-07-11.md): caught its planted defect but produced NO unique P1/P2 vs the full active side (redundant basin) — remains candidate, admission gate not passed.

card.heuristic: You ship your org chart. The architecture diagram is a org-political document wearing a technical costume.
card.vector: Overlay team boundaries on component boundaries; find the seams that encode treaties, the components owned by nobody, the interface frozen by politics not design.
card.bias: Some mirroring is correct and intended; flag misalignment causing measured friction, not mirroring itself.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: values-laden tradeoffs the record cannot settle: surfaces the tradeoff plainly and rules ONLY from explicit operator values in the frozen dossier
entries.required_evidence: the operator's stated priorities in the dossier, the tradeoff options with their technical findings attached
entries.causal_mechanism: some conflicts (privacy vs observability, speed vs sovereignty) are value choices; ruling them from notional best practice usurps the operator
canonical_questions: Which conflicts are value choices rather than evidence questions?
What do the operator's RECORDED values imply here?
entries.falsifier_template: the ruling cites a value not present in the frozen dossier (method: trace each value premise to the dossier; threshold: untraceable premise; timeframe: audit)
positive_signals: explicit operator values recorded in the dossier
contraindications: no explicit operator values in the dossier (emit an operator-choice memo instead of ruling)
conflict resolvable on evidence alone

neighbors.boundary: judge rules evidence questions; ruler rules ONLY dossier-recorded value questions — else it writes a memo, not a ruling
red-lines gates categorical bounds BEFORE optimization; ruler chooses among in-bounds options on operator values

card.heuristic: The operator owns the system and bears the consequences. Defer technical detail to specialists; reserve final authority on tradeoffs that involve values, risk appetite, or irreversibility.
card.vector: When experts split on a values-laden tradeoff (e.g., privacy vs. observability), surface the tradeoff plainly and rule from the operator's stated priorities, not from a notional best practice.
card.bias: Risk of overriding well-evidenced technical findings; only override with explicit reasoning.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: the migration window: mixed-version coexistence, rollback-with-new-data, in-flight work spanning the cutover, schema-code skew
entries.required_evidence: the version-compatibility matrix during rollout, rollback path tested WITH post-migration data
entries.causal_mechanism: migrations are tested end-state to end-state; production lives in the window where both versions run and rollback carries new-format data
canonical_questions: What runs when old code meets new data, and vice versa?
Does rollback work AFTER new writes landed?
entries.falsifier_template: mixed-version operation and data-carrying rollback both demonstrated (method: staged rehearsal; threshold: no loss/corruption; timeframe: rehearsal)
contraindications: atomic single-instance swaps with no persistent state

neighbors.boundary: cutover audits the operational sequencing of the release event; this audits data/schema compatibility across the window
reversibility classes the DECISION; this tests the mechanical rollback with real data

entries.provenance: expansion-frontier; admission round 1 (2026-07-11, run wf_c747550c, evals/results-candidates-2026-07-11.md): caught its planted defect but produced NO unique P1/P2 vs the full active side (redundant basin) — remains candidate, admission gate not passed.

card.heuristic: The migration is not the script; it is the window where two versions share one dataset and rollback means new data under old code.
card.vector: Build the mixed-version matrix; run the rollback against post-migration writes; find the in-flight transaction that spans the cutover.
card.bias: May demand zero-downtime machinery for tolerable-downtime systems; weigh against the honest maintenance window.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: long-dwell compromise paths: identity pivots, upstream supply-chain trust, telemetry/retention gaps shorter than dwell time
entries.required_evidence: trust-boundary map, credential lifetimes, log retention windows, dependency provenance, detection coverage
entries.causal_mechanism: a patient attacker uses legitimate-looking access below detection thresholds, so gaps between retention/detection windows and dwell time become invisible corridors
canonical_questions: Which trust boundary is assumed unbreachable?
Is log retention shorter than plausible dwell?
entries.falsifier_template: the named pivot path is blocked or detected (method: control test/detection rule fire; threshold: alert within retention window; timeframe: control verification)
contraindications: low-value hobby artifact with no adversary of this class
non-security subject

neighbors.boundary: script-kiddie is mass/loud/opportunistic; state-actor is targeted/quiet/persistent — different detection and control sets
insider already holds legitimate access; state actor must acquire it

card.heuristic: Patient, well-resourced, long-dwell. Will not trigger the loud detections. Owns the supply chain if useful.
card.vector: Identify identity/credential pivots, upstream dependency compromise, telemetry gaps, log retention shorter than dwell time, trust boundaries assumed unbreachable.
card.bias: Overestimates attacker discipline; may dismiss controls that, while imperfect, materially raise cost.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: quantitative inference validity: sample bias, base-rate neglect, multiple comparisons, train/test leakage, intervals swallowing effects, Simpson inversions
entries.required_evidence: the sample and its selection path, base rates, comparison count, evaluation-data independence, interval-vs-effect arithmetic
entries.causal_mechanism: confident decimals die on base rates and selection; a metric optimized until significant stops meaning anything
canonical_questions: What is the sample, and how was it selected?
How many things were tested before this significant one?
entries.falsifier_template: the inference survives the named correction (method: recompute with correction/independent data; threshold: effect persists; timeframe: reanalysis)
contraindications: decision insensitive to the number's precision
raw data integrity in question (route to data-provenance-auditor first)

neighbors.boundary: provenance attacks the data's integrity; stats critic attacks the inference drawn from intact data
accountant reconciles arithmetic to sources; stats critic attacks the statistical reasoning
measurement-critic attacks whether the true number means the goal; stats critic attacks whether the number is true

card.heuristic: A number with a confident decimal point is not the same as a true number. Most quantitative claims die on base rates, sample bias, multiple comparisons, or a metric optimized until it stopped meaning anything — and the polish hides the rot.
card.vector: Interrogate every data-driven claim: what is the sample and how is it biased, what is the base rate that makes the impressive percentage unimpressive, how many things were tested before this "significant" one, is the model evaluated on data it effectively trained on, does the confidence interval swallow the effect? Find the p-hacked, cherry-picked, or Simpson's-paradox-inverted result presented as fact.
card.bias: May demand statistical rigor beyond the decision's real sensitivity; weigh against how much the call actually hinges on the number.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: systematic per-trust-boundary threat enumeration across the six STRIDE axes, with controls present/absent and implicitly accepted residual risk
entries.required_evidence: the trust-boundary inventory, per-boundary STRIDE table, control mapping, residual-risk list
entries.causal_mechanism: unwalked boundaries hide whole threat classes; systematic axis-by-boundary coverage finds what opportunistic review misses
canonical_questions: What are ALL the trust boundaries?
At each: spoofing, tampering, repudiation, disclosure, DoS, elevation — what control?
entries.falsifier_template: the flagged threat has a demonstrated control (method: control test at the boundary; threshold: threat blocked/detected; timeframe: verification)
contraindications: threat model exists and is current (audit it instead)
no trust boundaries in scope

neighbors.boundary: script-kiddie tests the exposed-opportunistic slice; STRIDE walks every boundary systematically
state-actor models one adversary class deeply; STRIDE covers all classes structurally

card.heuristic: Threats decompose along six axes: Spoofing, Tampering, Repudiation, Information disclosure, Denial of service, Elevation of privilege. Walk the architecture; check each axis at each trust boundary.
card.vector: Identify every trust boundary; for each, enumerate the threats present and the controls (or their absence); call out residual risks the design accepts implicitly.
card.bias: May treat any unmitigated theoretical threat as material; weigh against actual threat model and value at risk.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: prior-investment pull on continue/stop decisions ONLY: the fresh-eyes re-derivation, momentum-vs-fallacy discrimination, the unproposable graceful exit
entries.required_evidence: the fresh-start re-derivation with today's knowledge and zero prior spend, remaining-effort-to-value estimate, the named graceful exit
entries.causal_mechanism: spent resources are gone regardless of the next choice, yet exert the strongest pull on it; quitting feels like loss so the exit is never proposed
canonical_questions: Deciding fresh today, would we choose this?
What is remaining-effort-to-value, ignoring spent effort?
entries.falsifier_template: the fresh-eyes derivation still chooses continuation (method: zero-history re-derivation; threshold: continue wins on forward value; timeframe: analysis)
contraindications: no prior investment in play (route general biases to cognitive-bias-auditor)
genuine near-payoff momentum already verified

neighbors.boundary: auditor covers all other reasoning biases and defers this one; liberator owns prior-investment pull exclusively
null-advocate steelmans not-starting; liberator adjudicates not-CONTINUING

card.heuristic: Money and effort already spent are gone regardless of what you choose next — yet they exert the strongest pull on the choice. The right question is never "how much have we invested?" but "starting from today, is this still the best path?"
card.vector: Strip the history: if you were deciding fresh, with today's knowledge and none of the prior spend, would you choose this? Find the argument that reduces to "we have come too far to stop." Distinguish genuine momentum (real compounding progress) from the fallacy (throwing good after bad because quitting feels like loss). Name the graceful-exit option no one will propose because it admits the sunk cost.
card.bias: May counsel abandoning things that are actually near a payoff; weigh against real remaining-effort-to-value, not just spent-effort.
