entries.object_of_scrutiny: feedback structure of the existing system: loops, delays, stocks/flows; symptomatic fixes that displace rather than resolve
entries.required_evidence: causal-loop or stock-flow sketch of the relevant structure, the displaced-symptom path, policy-resistance mechanism
entries.causal_mechanism: behavior is a property of structure; a local fix inside an unchanged loop produces the same behavior elsewhere after a delay
canonical_questions: Which loop produces this symptom, and does the fix touch the loop?
Where does the displaced symptom land?
entries.falsifier_template: the symptom stays resolved without reappearing elsewhere (method: monitor named displacement candidates; threshold: no displacement after fix; timeframe: post-fix window)
contraindications: genuinely local, structure-free defect
urgency that overrides root-cause work (declare, don't block)

neighbors.boundary: forecaster projects reactions to a NEW intervention; logician analyzes the EXISTING structure's behavior
ecological analyst maps the EXTERNAL environment; logician maps the internal feedback structure

card.heuristic: The behavior of a system is a property of its structure, not its components. Local fixes to systemic problems produce displaced symptoms.
card.vector: Trace feedback loops, delays, and stocks-and-flows; find places where the design solves a symptom while leaving its source intact; identify whether the proposed intervention will produce policy resistance.
card.bias: May resist any targeted intervention as "not addressing root causes"; weigh against urgency and reversibility.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: the debt ledger: which deferred decisions remain cheap to defer, which accrue compound interest, which transferred to a team that never took the loan
entries.required_evidence: per-debt servicing cost trend, interest evidence (slowdown, incident linkage), ownership transfer record
entries.causal_mechanism: debt is borrowed time, not sin; unpriced interest compounds silently until routine changes cost multiples of their size
canonical_questions: Which deferral is accruing compound interest right now?
Who is paying interest on a loan they never took?
entries.falsifier_template: the flagged debt's servicing cost is flat (method: measure change-cost trend; threshold: no compounding; timeframe: trend window)
contraindications: debt already priced and scheduled
greenfield with no deferred decisions

neighbors.boundary: entropy hunts UNOWNED decay nobody chose; curator prices decisions someone DID defer
architect steers new decisions; curator triages the existing ledger

card.heuristic: Tech debt is not a moral failing; it is borrowed time. The question is whether the interest is being paid and whether the loan term is acceptable.
card.vector: Identify which deferred decisions are still cheap to defer, which are accruing compound interest, and which have moved to a different team that didn't take out the loan.
card.bias: May moralize about debt that's correctly priced; weigh against actual servicing cost.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: perceived-quality surface detail: microcopy, transitions, empty states, error recovery affordances, jank
entries.required_evidence: screen-by-screen walkthrough, empty/error state inventory, copy audit, interaction latency
entries.causal_mechanism: users judge competence by surface quality; jank and blame-the-user errors signal fragility regardless of core correctness
canonical_questions: Which error message blames the user?
Which empty state is a dead-end?
entries.falsifier_template: the flagged surface passes a naive-user impression test (method: walkthrough; threshold: next action always evident; timeframe: session)
contraindications: workflow itself broken (route angry-customer/adoption-realist first)
no visual surface

neighbors.boundary: angry-customer attacks flow dead-ends and traps adversarially; polisher refines surface detail constructively
WCAG expert audits against a conformance standard; polisher targets subjective perceived quality

card.heuristic: Users judge competence by surface quality. The 80/20 of perceived quality lives in microcopy, transitions, empty states, and error recovery.
card.vector: Find dead-ends without next-action affordance, error messages that blame the user, jank that signals fragility, dark patterns that erode trust, accessibility holes that exclude users.
card.bias: May over-polish surfaces of workflows that are themselves broken; weigh against fundamental task fit.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: per-unit marginal economics at real volume, including forgotten costs (egress, support, human-in-loop, retry amplification) and hidden cross-subsidies
entries.required_evidence: cost per request/user/transaction computed from primary figures, volume projections, subsidy sources
entries.causal_mechanism: demo economics are subsidized; if marginal cost exceeds marginal revenue, growth accelerates the bleed rather than fixing it
canonical_questions: What does one marginal unit really cost, all-in?
Where does growth make the math worse?
entries.falsifier_template: computed per-unit margin is positive at target volume (method: reconciled cost model; threshold: margin >= 0 including hidden costs; timeframe: projection)
contraindications: deliberately funded loss-leader with an explicit payback thesis
no meaningful per-unit cost structure

neighbors.boundary: accountant reconciles claimed figures to sources; unit-economics computes forward viability
opportunity-cost weighs the best alternative use of resources; unit-economics weighs this path's own margin

card.heuristic: It works in the demo because the demo is subsidized. At scale, every unit either makes money or bleeds it, and hope is not a business model. The interesting number is marginal cost, not total cost.
card.vector: Compute the per-unit economics at real volume — cost per request/user/transaction including the parts everyone forgets (egress, support load, the human in the loop, the retry amplification). Find where growth makes the math *worse*, not better. Identify the cross-subsidy that hides the loss.
card.bias: May kill strategically-loss-leading investments; weigh against deliberate, funded subsidy with a payback thesis.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: whether to decide or learn: what evidence would change the decision, what it costs to get, whether the cheap discriminating test is being skipped — or deliberation is continuing past the point information could matter
entries.required_evidence: the decision-relevant uncertainty list, per-uncertainty resolution cost and decision impact, the cheapest discriminator
entries.causal_mechanism: teams decide when they should learn and deliberate when nothing learnable would change the choice; both waste the same resource
canonical_questions: What observation would actually change this decision?
What is the cheapest test that discriminates between the live options?
entries.falsifier_template: the identified test discriminates at stated cost (method: run/spec the discriminator; threshold: outcome maps to different choices; timeframe: test)
contraindications: uncertainty already irreducible or decision-insensitive

neighbors.boundary: robust-decision picks among options under uncertainty; this asks whether to BUY uncertainty down first
premise-auditor finds the shaky assumption; this prices the test that would check it

entries.provenance: expansion-frontier; admission round 1 (2026-07-11, run wf_c747550c, evals/results-candidates-2026-07-11.md): caught its planted defect but produced NO unique P1/P2 vs the full active side (redundant basin) — remains candidate, admission gate not passed.

card.heuristic: Half of deliberation is buying information that is on sale somewhere for a tenth of the price. The other half is buying information that cannot change the answer.
card.vector: List the uncertainties; for each: would resolving it flip the decision, and what does resolution cost; sequence the cheap discriminators first.
card.bias: May defer decisions whose delay cost exceeds any information value; price the waiting too.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: oracle adequacy: whether the passing check actually exercises the claimed behavior — mocks mirroring bugs, tests asserting the implementation, green-but-vacuous suites
entries.required_evidence: the claim-to-oracle mapping: what each verification actually executes and observes vs what the claim asserts
entries.causal_mechanism: a check can pass while exercising nothing relevant; success claims inherit the oracle's blind spots invisibly
canonical_questions: Does the green check exercise the asserted behavior end-to-end?
What bug class would this suite pass right through?
entries.falsifier_template: the oracle detects a planted violation of the claim (method: mutation/planted-flaw test; threshold: check fails on plant; timeframe: test)
contraindications: claims verified by direct observation, not proxy checks

neighbors.boundary: stats critic attacks inference from data; this attacks the checking apparatus itself
epistemic grades evidence status; this tests whether the evidence-generator can even see the failure

entries.provenance: expansion-frontier; admission round 1 (2026-07-11, run wf_c747550c, evals/results-candidates-2026-07-11.md): caught its planted defect but produced NO unique P1/P2 vs the full active side (redundant basin) — remains candidate, admission gate not passed.; RETIRED via consolidation 2026-07-14: admission round 1 (evals/results-candidates-2026-07-11.md) found its object (oracle adequacy) fully covered by EVIDENCE-1 + the panel; converted to WORKFLOW DISCIPLINE — the Step 6 mechanical-criticism oracle-adequacy check in SKILL.md. Nearest absorber lens: forensic-accountant.

card.heuristic: A green check proves the check passed, not that the claim is true. The oracle's blind spots become your blind spots the moment you trust it.
card.vector: Map each claim to what its check actually observes; plant a violation and see if anything goes red; hunt the mock that mirrors the bug.
card.bias: Mutation-style rigor everywhere is unaffordable; spend it on the claims that gate irreversible action.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: WCAG 2.1 AA conformance: keyboard nav, contrast, focus order, alt text, ARIA, error identification, timing independence — easy-fix vs structural classification
entries.required_evidence: per-criterion audit results, the specific failing elements, easy/structural classification
entries.causal_mechanism: inaccessible interfaces exclude users structurally and silently; conformance criteria make the exclusion enumerable and fixable
canonical_questions: Which WCAG 2.1 AA criteria fail, on which elements?
Which failures are structural vs one-line fixes?
entries.falsifier_template: the flagged element passes the criterion (method: re-test with AT/tooling; threshold: criterion met; timeframe: retest)
contraindications: no interface surface
expert-only internal tool where operator explicitly accepts the gap (record it)

neighbors.boundary: polisher targets subjective perceived quality; WCAG expert audits objective conformance criteria

card.heuristic: Accessibility is not a feature; it is a property of a usable system. If the interface excludes users, it is broken for everyone, eventually.
card.vector: Audit against WCAG 2.1 AA: keyboard navigation, contrast, focus order, alt text, ARIA, error identification, timing-independent operation; identify which failures are easy fixes vs. structural.
card.bias: Treats accessibility gaps as equally urgent regardless of user impact; weigh against actual user base.

entries.object_of_scrutiny: human capacity arithmetic: on-call load, toil growth vs headcount, single-person critical paths, the burnout trajectory in the plan's assumptions
entries.required_evidence: per-person load accounting (on-call frequency, toil hours, interrupt rates), trend vs capacity
entries.causal_mechanism: plans budget machines and forget humans; sustained overload converts to attrition, and attrition converts to bus-factor collapse exactly when load peaks
canonical_questions: What does this plan assume about human hours, and is it survivable?
Whose calendar is the hidden critical path?
entries.falsifier_template: the load arithmetic stays under sustainable thresholds (method: compute per-person load; threshold: within stated sustainable bounds; timeframe: projection)
contraindications: automation-complete operations with no human load question

neighbors.boundary: bus-factor is knowledge concentration; this is workload concentration and its attrition dynamics
unit-economics prices the service; this prices the humans the service consumes

entries.provenance: expansion-frontier; admission round 1 (2026-07-11, run wf_c747550c, evals/results-candidates-2026-07-11.md): caught its planted defect but produced NO unique P1/P2 vs the full active side (redundant basin) — remains candidate, admission gate not passed.

card.heuristic: The plan works if nobody sleeps. Human capacity is the resource every roadmap spends and no roadmap budgets.
card.vector: Do the per-person arithmetic: on-call nights, interrupt load, toil growth against headcount; find the person whose exit collapses the plan.
card.bias: May treat all load as damage; distinguish sustainable stretch from compounding overload.
